title: Charity founded by Sarah Ferguson shuts down after release of Epstein emails with former duchess
plaintext:
The charity founded by Sarah Ferguson, the former Duchess of York, is shutting down following the release of emails showing the depth of her friendship with the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

Sarah’s Trust, which focused on improving the lives of women and children, said it will close for the “foreseeable future,’’ following the revelations in latest documents released by the U.S. Department of Justice. Ferguson is the ex-wife of the former Prince Andrew, who was stripped of his royal titles due to his own links with Epstein.

“Our chair Sarah Ferguson and the board of trustees have agreed that with regret the charity will shortly close for the foreseeable future,’’ the trust said in a statement late on Monday. “This has been under discussion and in train for some months.’’

Emails released on Friday revealed that Ferguson remained in contact with Epstein long after his 2008 conviction for soliciting prostitution from an underage girl. In the email exchanges, Ferguson referred to Epstein as a “legend’’ and “the brother I have always wished for.’’

In one exchange, from March 2011, Epstein complained about a story in London’s Evening Standard newspaper in which he was identified as a pedophile and Ferguson said she had “deep regret” over her ties to Epstein.

The following month, someone identified as “Sarah” emailed Epstein saying she “did not” and “would not” call him a “P” and that she had acted to “protect my own brand.”

title: Lebanon, Jordan PMs discuss energy cooperation in Dubai
plaintext:
Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam met Jordanian Prime Minister Jafar Hassan on the sidelines of the World Governments Summit in Dubai, where the two leaders discussed ways to strengthen bilateral cooperation and reviewed regional developments.

During the meeting, the Jordanian side proposed an initiative to hold a trilateral Syrian-Lebanese-Jordanian meeting in Amman dedicated to energy and electricity issues. Salam welcomed the proposal, viewing it as a potential step toward enhancing regional coordination in vital sectors.

The two sides also agreed to organize a conference for Jordanian and Lebanese business leaders in April to boost economic ties and encourage private-sector cooperation between the two countries.

Regional developments were also discussed during the talks, reflecting shared concerns about evolving political and economic challenges.

title: Wildcats’ double act Greg Hire and Damian Martin hit Shire of Gnowangerup with clinics and tips for local kids
plaintext:
About 70 children from Ongerup, Borden and Gnowangerup had their basketball dreams come true as former Perth Wildcats stars Greg Hire and Damian Martin conducted a series of clinics to inspire young people across the region.

The pair delivered five sessions over January 29 and 30 to participants aged from five to 21, with three clinics taking place in Gnowangerup and one apiece in Ongerup and Borden.

Beyond basketball skills, the athletes shared powerful messages about resilience, goal-setting, and perseverance, encouraging young people not to let living in a regional community limit their ambitions.

Following each clinic, both players stayed back to chat with participants, sign merchandise, take photos, and answer questions.

On the evening of January 29, the Gnowangerup community came together for an open mic event, giving residents the opportunity to ask questions and hear stories from the pair about their professional basketball careers.

Thanks to catering provided by the Roast & Brew Cafe, $810 was raised for St John WA’s Gnowangerup sub-centre.

The tour concluded on Friday night with a community barbecue in Borden, where participants were given an insight into the world of elite basketball.

The clinics and get-togethers were part of the Shire of Gnowangerup’s Youth Engagement Plan and supported by the WA Government through a community place-based sport and recreation grant.

title: Witness F seeks to block his appearance before Madlanga commission
plaintext:
Witness F, who was meant to appear before the Madlanga commission on Tuesday, has brought an application to interdict his appearance.

The commission is probing allegations of criminality, political interference and corruption in the criminal justice system.

Witness F’s lawyer, Hartley Ngoato, arrived at the commission in Pretoria at 9:34am.

Sowetan understands that Witness F is one of the police officials who was the middleman between alleged cartel members and suspended deputy commissioner Shadrack Sibiya.

This is a developing story.

Sowetan

title: Emma Raducanu bounces back from split with coach to advance at Winners Open
plaintext:
Emma Raducanu bounced back from her split with coach Francisco Roig to ease past Greet Minnen in straight-sets at the Winners Open in Romania.

The 2021 US Open champion suffered a disappointing 7-6 (3) 6-2 defeat to Amanda Potapova in the second round of the Australian Open last month.

Raducanu’s partnership with Rafael Nadal’s old coach Roig subsequently ended after her early exit in Melbourne, but the top seed in Transylvania produced an accomplished display to get the better of Minnen by a 6-0 6-4 score.

In a comprehensive showing, Raducanu sent down three aces and forced four breaks of serve across 70 minutes of action.

The 23-year-old will face Kaja Juvan of Slovenia next in the second round of the WTA 250 event on Wednesday.

Raducanu, whose father is Romanian, said in her on-court interview: “I’m very happy.

“I think I played a great match, super aggressive from the beginning and the crowd from the minute I walked out was incredible so thank you very much for all the support.

“Today I think was a great performance. I really wanted to put my game out on the court, it’s a little bit easier indoors so I am really pleased I could use that.”
sentence: In a comprehensive showing, Raducanu sent down three aces and forced four breaks of serve across 70 minutes of action.

title: City Beach crash: Man killed after car rolls and bursts into flames on Underwood Avenue
plaintext:
A man has died after a car rolled and burst into flames in City Beach during peak hour traffic.

Emergency services were called to Underwood Avenue about 6.50pm on Friday after a beige Toyota Camry travelling west struck the central median strip near Perry Lakes Drive.

The vehicle rolled before catching fire, prompting a major response from Police, St John WA and the Department of Fire and Emergency Services.

The 48-year-old male passenger died at the scene from his injuries.

The driver, a 58-year-old man, was pulled from the wreckage with critical injuries and rushed to Royal Perth Hospital for treatment.

Police are continuing to investigate the circumstances leading up to the crash and are urging anyone who witnessed the incident, or who has dashcam or mobile phone vision, to come forward.

Information can be provided to Crime Stoppers. Vision relating to the crash can also be uploaded directly to investigators via this link.

title: Urgent search for 71-year-old bushwalker missing in the Blue Mountains
plaintext:
An urgent search is in its second day for a bushwalker who vanished in the Blue Mountains.

About 5.30am Monday, David Elliot, 71, told his family he would be completing a 30km bushwalk at the Dunphys Camp Ground in Megalong Valley in the Blue Mountains.

The alarm was raised when he failed to return home at 7.30pm the same day.

Crews from the NSW SES, Rural Fire Service (RFS) and police rescue launched a major search for the missing bushwalker, spanning the area until 11.30pm Monday.

The search for the missing bushwalker resumed at 9,30am Tuesday, police confirmed.

While David is considered an “experienced” bushwalker, he was not appropriately dressed to protect himself for overnight weather conditions.

“Police and family hold concerns for his welfare due to the weather and rugged terrain,” a police statement read.

“He was reported to have a Personal Locator Beach (PLB), but this has not been activated.”

David is described as being caucasian in appearance, about 180cm tall with a medium build and bald.

He was last seen wearing beige pants and a black or beige shirt, with a beige-coloured floppy hat and glasses.

Anyone with information about his whereabouts are urged to contact police or Crime Stoppers on 1800 333 000.

title: Mountain lion spotted in Northern California days after another was captured
plaintext:
A mountain lion was spotted in California wine country Sunday, just days after another puma had been captured in San Francisco.

A video shared by North Bay Animal Services showed the lion walking through a backyard in Sebastopol, a small city in Sonoma County about 50 miles north of San Francisco, just feet from a frightened resident who filmed the encounter.

Multiple residents reported seeing the mountain lion Sunday morning, the Sebastopol Police Department said in a statement.

“If you live in that vicinity it would be wise to bring any pets inside,” the police department wrote.

The animal had not been captured by Monday, according to police.

Five days earlier, animal control officers in San Francisco captured a mountain lion within the city after an hours-long standoff.

The lion had been seen traversing the city and probably got lost, San Francisco Animal Care and Control said in a Facebook post.

California once had specialists dedicated to resolving conflict between people and wolves, mountain lions and coyotes. But after funding ran dry in 2024, the state let all but one of them go.

Now, a coalition of wildlife advocates is calling for the state to bring back, expand and fund the coexistence program, at roughly $15 million annually.

Times staff writer Lila Seidman contributed to this report.

title: Local star injured at the wrong time
plaintext:
West Coast are expecting defender Brady Hough to miss around a month with the ankle injury he sustained earlier this week.

Hough limped from the training track on Thursday after going down in a contested marking drill and was promptly sent for scans on the injury, which the Eagles believe will sideline him for three to four weeks.

The timeline scuppers Hough’s hopes of featuring for WA in their AFL Origin clash with Victoria at Optus Stadium on February 14 but means he remains a chance to push for selection in West Coast’s sole official pre-season fixture against Port Adelaide on March 1.

Drafted out of Harvey-Brunswick-Leschenault, Hough has long been earmarked as a big part of West Coast’s rebuild, having signed on at the club until the end of 2028.

The Eagles will open their 2026 campaign with a trip to Queensland to take on Gold Coast on March 15.

Hough impressed at stages after being moved into the midfield for the back half of West Coast’s one-win campaign last year but is set for a return to defence this season.

He had already endured an interrupted pre-season, missing the club’s sessions last week due to illness.

The 22-year-old has been earmarked for an important role in an Eagles back line which will be without retired vice-captain Jeremy McGovern but added Tylar Young from Richmond over the off-season.

title: Jaxon Jai Trinidad: Samurai sword charge ends in $500 fine after grievous bodily harm charge dropped
plaintext:
A young father who was accused of cutting his dad’s arm off with a samurai sword, before having the charge dropped, has been fined $500 and given a spent conviction order for his remaining weapon charges.

Jaxon Jai Trinidad, 20, fronted the Kalgoorlie Magistrates Court on Monday and pleaded guilty to one count of carrying or possessing an edged weapon, and two counts of possessing a controlled weapon.

Defence lawyer Max Crisp told the court his client previously had a charge of aggravated grievous bodily harm, which was dropped by the Director of Public Prosecutions late last year.

He said the charge related to an altercation between Trinidad — aged 19 at the time — and his estranged father on February 19, 2025, when he cut off his dad’s arm with a samurai sword.

Mr Crisp said the father and son had a “horrible” relationship, and detailed how the dad was a drug user, before Magistrate Donna Webb told the lawyer to stop talking.

Prosecutor Sen. Const. Carl Logan told the court the standing charges related to police seizing multiple weapons on February 20, 2025, from under a bed in Trinidad’s Piccadilly home, including the double-edged samurai sword.

Mr Crisp said Trinidad’s intentions with the weapons were to eventually display them on his wall.

Ms Webb approved a spent conviction order for Trinidad and issued the $500 fine.

Ms Webb approved a spent conviction order for Trinidad and issued the $500 fine.

title: Antiques and collectables fill Brunswick’s Town Hall
plaintext:
From glassware to memorabilia, there was something unique around every corner at Brunswick’s Town Hall at the weekend.

For the first time, an antique and collectors fair was held in Brunswick, with the event spanning across Saturday and Sunday.

Event organiser from WA Antique Fairs Warren Gibbs said antique fairs were regularly held in the South West, including in Harvey, Bunbury, Busselton and Bridgetown.

He said the fair made its debut in Brunswick after he was approached by a local seller.

“They said ‘you need to try and organise one at Brunswick because it’s a big beautiful hall’,” Mr Gibbs said.

He said the fair included anything and everything.

“You’ve got jewellery, sports memorabilia, tools, china, opals, rocks, glassware, crystal, everything,” he said.

Now running about 15 antique fairs a year, Mr Gibbs said he was a previous seller who became involved after a fair organiser in Southern River decided to retire.

“A lot of the sellers were a bit upset because it was a pretty good little fair,” he said.

“She said ‘Would you like to take it over?’.

“So I started with that one and then people were saying ‘Have you thought about organising one here or there?’.”

Mr Gibbs said there would be about seven more antique fairs hosted in the South West later this year.

title: Disposal site in Waroona has been temporarily closed following a hazardous fire
plaintext:
A hazardous fire has caused the temporary closure of the Buller Road refuse disposal site in the shire of Waroona.

A Department of Fire and Emergency Services spokesperson said a triple-0 call was received just before 4.30pm on Saturday reporting a rubbish a fire at a waste facility in Waroona.

Spreading to nearby bushland, they said the fire was the result of a flare-up from a previous fire.

“Fourteen appliances attended, with volunteer crews working to bring the fire under control by 7.49pm, with the assistance of three aircraft,” they said.

“A hazmat warning was issued as the fire was burning in a general waste pile, resulting in potentially hazardous smoke in the area.”

The spokesperson said the incident was being managed by the Shire of Waroona, with crews conducting patrols in the area.

In an online post on February 2, the shire said the site would be assessed daily before it reopened, and it encouraged residents to monitor shire communication channels for updates.

The shire thanked all those involved in helping combat the fire, including the Waroona Volunteer Fire and Emergency Service and surrounding brigades.

title: Tourist trampled to death by elephant in Thai national park
plaintext:
A wild bull elephant trampled and killed a 65-year-old tourist at a national park in Thailand on Monday, according to park officials.

The tourist was taking a morning walk with his wife when an elephant named Oyewan trampled him at Khao Yai National Park, national park chief Chaiya Huayhongthong told AFP.

Park rangers managed to scare the animal away, which allowed the tourist’s wife to escape, Chaiya said.

"He was the third person killed by Oyewan," he said, adding that authorities will meet on Friday to decide on how to deal with the elephant.

"We will probably decide to relocate him or change his behavior," he told the outlet, without elaborating.

The tourist killed was identified as Jirathachai Jiraphatboonyathorn from Lopburi province, the Bangkok Post reported.

Fox News Digital reached out to Khao Yai National Park but did not immediately hear back.

Wild elephants have caused an estimated 227 human fatalities between 2012 and 2024 in Thailand, The Nation reported, citing a report from the Department of National Parks, Wildlife, and Plant Conservation.

The elephant population in Thailand has ballooned since 2015, rising from 334 to nearly 800 last year, AFP reported. Authorities have been trying to manage the population by giving female elephants contraceptive vaccines.

title: Teens accessed ‘extremist material’, ballistic vest seized: police
plaintext:
Two teenagers have been charged and a ballistic-style vest and extremist literature seized following a terror probe in a small rural rown.

NSW Police raided a home in Moree, about 120km south of the Queensland border, late in March following reports in December that a teenager had allegedly accessed extremist material online.

A 15-year-old boy was charged after a mobile phone, a ballistic-style vest, and several notebooks were seized.

The items were subsequently analysed by police and the matter was referred to the NSW Joint Counter Terrorism Team, which carried out further raids at two homes in Moree on Thursday.

Police allegedly located and seized a number of handwritten notes and pieces of literature alleged to contain extremist references during the operation.

The same 15-year-old was arrested at 10am and taken to Moree police station.

He has since been charged with using a carriage service to transmit violent extremist material and knowingly collecting or making a document connected with terrorism.

A 16-year-old boy was also arrested later that morning.

He has been charged with using a carriage service to possess violent extremist material.

Both teens were refused bail to appear before Parramatta children’s court on Friday.

title: Thousands report outage issues with Commonwealth Bank’s internet banking app
plaintext:
Thousands of Commonwealth Bank Australia customers have reported experiencing issues logging on to their internet banking app.

More than 2600 reports of outages or problems were flagged with Downdetector.net on Wednesday night.

The most reported problems – 75 per cent of reports – stemmed from issues with the app, according to the website.

Complaints have also started to stream in on Commonwealth Bank’s Facebook page, with some posting images of being locked out of the app.

A CBA spokeswoman clarified the issues were “intermittent” and coverage was returning - telling NewsWire the issue had been resolved at 8.40pm.

Similar messages emerged in the bank’s Facebook comments - one message to a customer reading: “We are aware that some customers were experiencing some intermittent issues with the CommBank app and NetBank. All services are currently available and we apologise and appreciate your patience”.

More to come.

title: King Charles to address US Congress during visit
plaintext:
King Charles will address the US Congress on April 28 during his visit to Washington DC, House Speaker Mike Johnson says.

Charles and his wife Queen Camilla will visit the United States in a long-planned trip to mark the 250th anniversary of that country's independence from British rule, before the couple pay a visit to Bermuda, Buckingham Palace announced on Tuesday.

The high-profile trip comes as the United Kingdom government hopes to repair relations with US President Donald Trump, which have been damaged by the Iran war.

It will be the first state visit by a UK monarch since 2007, when Charles' mother Queen Elizabeth made what was the fourth such US trip of her reign.

Trump said the King and Queen's visit would begin on April 27 with a banquet dinner at the White House the next day.

title: Dozens of cattle, man in 20s die in crash near Tamworth
plaintext:
A man in his 20s and dozens of cattle have died in a three-vehicle crash in country NSW.

The crash happened east of Tamworth on Wednesday morning, and police investigators will try to piece together what caused the ute and two B-double trucks to crash.

The crash happened on a sealed arterial road off the main highway in the area of Walcha, about 90km east of Tamworth, about 10am on Wednesday.

When police and emergency services arrived, they found a ute had been involved in a crash with one B-double loaded with cattle and another unloaded B-double.

A 22-year-old man driving the cattle truck died at the scene, a police spokesperson said.

The 53-year-old man driving the second truck was taken to hospital with serious head injuries and for mandatory testing. The 63-year-old ute driver suffered minor injuries, and he too was subject to mandatory testing.

About 35 cattle died in the crash, some having to be euthanised because of their injuries, the spokesperson said.

Police want anyone with dashcam or phone footage, or information about the crash, to come forward.

title: Man arrested at Melbourne Airport and charged with ex-partner’s murder
plaintext:
A man has been arrested and charged with the alleged murder of his former partner as he attempted to board a flight at Melbourne Airport.

Eva Lasrini’s body was discovered at Little River, south-west of Melbourne, on Saturday, two days after she was reported missing by relatives.

Her former partner Allan Keys, 67, was arrested on Friday afternoon as he attempted to board an international flight.

Police launched an investigation and her disappearance was deemed suspicious after she failed to make contact with relatives.

Her body was discovered on Saturday near Little River Rd and the Princes Freeway, which links Melbourne to Geelong.

Mr Keys has been charged with one count of murder.

He faced Melbourne Magistrate’s Court on Sunday and was remanded in custody to next appear in court in August.

title: WAFL 2026: Next Generation Academy prospects Garrison Kenh and Finlay Yeo start colts season in style
plaintext:
A pair of Next Generation Academy prospects tied to West Coast and Fremantle starred in the first round of the WAFL colts season.

Eagles-linked rebounding defender Garrison Kenh racked up 25 disposals in East Perth’s 18-point win over Subiaco on Good Friday, after Dockers-tied excitement machine Finlay Yeo notched a team-high 24 disposals and applied six tackles as East Fremantle beat Peel Thunder by 49 points on Thursday.

The left-footed Kenh will spend parts of this year among the company of the country’s best draft prospects as a member of the AFL Academy and was a regular participant in West Coast training over the summer.

Last season, Yeo won the competition’s mark of the year and goal of the year awards inside the same game and Fremantle’s NGA coach Roger Hayden has expressed excitement over his aerial ability and clean skills.

Benji Van Rooyen — the brother of Melbourne star Jacob and one of WA’s four AFL Academy members — was everywhere in Claremont’s seven-point victory over West Perth in Joondalup on Saturday, stuffing the stat sheet with 26 disposals, 16 hit-outs and a goal.

Rugged on-baller Leo Steed shone for Swan Districts despite a 29-point loss to South Fremantle, leading all comers with 31 disposals and eight tackles.

Rugged on-baller Leo Steed shone for Swan Districts despite a 29-point loss to South Fremantle, leading all comers with 31 disposals and eight tackles.

title: Secret Service investigates reports of gunfire near Lafayette Park across from the White House
plaintext:
WASHINGTON — The U.S. Secret Service said Sunday that it was investigating reports of overnight gunfire near Lafayette Park, which is across the street from the White House.

No injuries were reported and no suspect was found after a search of the park and the surrounding area after midnight, the agency said in an online post.

President Trump was spending the weekend at the White House, which had no immediate comment on the incident. White House operations remained as normal but security in the area was increased, according to the Secret Service.

The park has been fenced off for weeks of renovations.

The Secret Service said it was working with the D.C. Metropolitan Police Department and U.S. Park Police.

title: Sweden to buy air defence systems for 8.7bn crowns, defence minister says
plaintext:
Sweden will buy air defence and anti-drone systems worth 8.7-billion Swedish crowns ($916-million) from among others Saab and BAE Systems, the country's defence minister said on Thursday.

"It is more important than ever to invest in air defence capabilities," Pal Jonson said, in a statement. "Protection against threats from the air is a high priority for the government and we will conclude more agreements to strengthen the Swedish defence."

The package will include anti-drone system Gute II, a radar and cannon system that can be mounted stationary or on vehicles. It is designed to protect military units as well as critical infrastructure, such as nuclear power plants or railway junctions.

The money will also be used for ammunition and vehicles, including infantry mobility vehicles from Finish defence firm Sisu.

Deliveries will take place in 2027 and 2028, Jonson said.

Sweden, like most European countries, is racing to bolster its defence following Russia's invasion of Ukraine and military spending is projected to be 2.8% of GDP in 2026, rising to 3.5% in 2030.

($1 = 9.4946 Swedish crowns)

(Reporting by Johan Ahlander; editing by Niklas Pollard)

title: Cosmos bloom at Delta Park [PICS & VIDEO]
plaintext:
The Cosmos flowers have started to bloom in Delta Park and many nature lovers are making their way to these fields to witness the seasonal marvel.

Autumn is upon us, and that means that the Cosmos flowers are making their return to Delta Park in Craighall.

These pink, purple and white flowers can typically be seen in parts of the Highveld from mid March to May.

Cosmos typically bloom in fields and roadside verges bringing colour to parts of the Gauteng and the Free State.

Where to see the Cosmos flowers

These colourful flowers were accidently brought from Argentina, to South Africa during the Anglo-Boer War when horse feed was contaminated with seeds.

Since then nature lovers have always looked for these locations to take photographs, picnic and hike through these seasonal marvels.

Although Delta Park is one of the more popular locations to see the Cosmos along the bike trails and dog-walking paths, they can also be found in Lanseria, Magaliesburg and the Cradle of Humankind.

title: Holiday-makers urged to stay ‘shark smart’ during South West salmon run
plaintext:
Holiday-makers looking to hit the water this Easter are being urged to remain vigilant during the Autumn salmon run – with the school of fish known to attract sharks, sea lions and dolphins.

According to the Department of Primary Industries and Regional Development, salmon venture south during their westward migration to spawn, and often congregate along popular surf beaches.

DPIRD shark response unit manager Michael Burke said fishers could help keep beaches safer by “bleeding” their catch away from the water’s edge and disposing of all fish waste responsibly.

“Large schools of salmon provide an exciting fishing opportunity, but swimmers, surfers and other ocean users should avoid areas where large schools of fish are present,” he said.

“It’s a good idea to be aware of the surroundings and take note of wildlife behaving erratically as a sign there may be large schools of fish nearby.”

Mr Burke said Surf Life Saving WA helicopters would continue to operate daily during the school holidays along the South West coast.

If the helicopter hovers and sounds a siren, swimmers need to leave the water immediately for at least one hour.

“Beach users can download the free SharkSmart WA app or visit the SharkSmart website to stay informed of shark activity and latest warnings,” Mr Burke said.

“All water users should report shark sightings to water police immediately on 9442 8600.”
sentence: Holiday-makers looking to hit the water this Easter are being urged to remain vigilant during the Autumn salmon run – with the school of fish known to attract sharks, sea lions and dolphins.

According to the Department of Primary Industries and Regional Development, salmon venture south during their westward migration to spawn, and often congregate along popular surf beaches.

DPIRD shark response unit manager Michael Burke said fishers could help keep beaches safer by “bleeding” their catch away from the water’s edge and disposing of all fish waste responsibly.

“Large schools of salmon provide an exciting fishing opportunity, but swimmers, surfers and other ocean users should avoid areas where large schools of fish are present,” he said.

“It’s a good idea to be aware of the surroundings and take note of wildlife behaving erratically as a sign there may be large schools of fish nearby.”

Mr Burke said Surf Life Saving WA helicopters would continue to operate daily during the school holidays along the South West coast.

If the helicopter hovers and sounds a siren, swimmers need to leave the water immediately for at least one hour.

“Beach users can download the free SharkSmart WA app or visit the SharkSmart website to stay informed of shark activity and latest warnings,” Mr Burke said.

“All water users should report shark sightings to water police immediately on 9442 8600.”

title: 24-year-old Bhutanese man who allegedly held $78,590 of stolen cash in garbage bags pleads not guilty
plaintext:
A 24-year-old man who allegedly possessed two garbage bags stuffed full of thousands of dollars in stolen cash will plead his case after his lawyer assured the court he has “an explanation”.

Kelzang Dorji appeared in Katanning Magistrates Court on March 24 charged with possession of stolen or unlawfully obtained property.

He pleaded not guilty to the single charge.

The court was told that on December 12, 2025, in Beaufort River, Mr Dorji allegedly had the care of two black garbage bags.

They held a collective total of $78,590 in Australian cash.

His lawyer Ken Bates told the court his client has “got an explanation” and the matter could be dealt with in a magistrate court trial.

Mr Bates recommended a full-day trial, but police prosecutor Sgt Dylan Fisher suggested a half-day after looking over the evidence.

He said Mr Dorji would require a Dzongkha interpreter.

Dzongkha is the national language of Bhutan.

Magistrate Rosemarie Myers said she had “grave concerns” with the court’s ability to find a Dzongkha interpreter because it had failed to do so for previous matters unrelated to Mr Dorji.

Ms Myers proposed finding an acquaintance of Mr Dorji’s to interpret as a back-up, despite not being a registered court interpreter.

The accused will next appear in Katanning Magistrates Court on October 12 for a trial.

title: Rare Ferrari Monza SP2 heads to Villa d’Este auction
plaintext:
A low-mileage example of the Ferrari Monza SP2 will go under the hammer at the Concorso d’Eleganza Villa d’Este auction on May 16–17, hosted by Broad Arrow Auctions.

Revealed in 2018 alongside the single-seat SP1, the SP2 forms part of Ferrari’s limited-run Icona series, which draws inspiration from historic models such as the Ferrari 166 MM Barchetta, 750 Monza and 860 Monza.

Unlike the SP1, the SP2 features two seats and a second headrest fairing for a passenger.

The SP2 is based on the Ferrari 812 Superfast and uses the same 6.5l naturally aspirated V12 engine. At launch, this unit produced 587kW and 719Nm, enabling a claimed 0–100km/h time of 2.9 seconds.

The example on offer, chassis No 261538, has covered just 417km. It is finished in a two-tone paint scheme combining Rosso California with a Grigio Coburn nose section and Argento Nürburgring accents.

Like other Monza models, the SP2 does not have a conventional windscreen. Instead, it uses Ferrari’s “Virtual Windshield” system, which channels airflow over the occupants. The car also features an aerodynamic package designed to improve stability and downforce.

Inside, the cabin is trimmed in Jeans Aunde Blu fabric and Pelle Elmo Blue leather, with embroidered Ferrari logos on the headrests. It is equipped with four-point safety harnesses and is supplied with a pair of carbon-fibre helmets by Berluti.

title: WA FuelWatch: See the list of service stations with no petrol or diesel
plaintext:
As the fuel crisis continues, there’s a fresh list of the service stations across the State that have run out of fuel.

According to FuelWatch website, there are 9 service stations across the State that have no diesel and no petrol on Tuesday.

There are 13 WA service stations that do not have any petrol (counted as empty if they don’t have either unleaded or premium unleaded) and 27 service stations that are without diesel (counted as empty if they don’t have either diesel or brand diesel).

Yesterday there were 14 service stations that did not have any petrol and 41 service stations that are without diesel across the State.

title: Lifeblood call for more donors as 1300 more blood donations needed in WA this Easter long weekend
plaintext:
Red Cross Lifeblood is urgently calling on people with type O and A blood to donate as reserves face significant pressure this Easter.

More than 1300 blood donations are needed in WA between 31 March and 10 April to ensure blood is available for people who need it.

With fuel supply disruptions also impacting the ability for some donors to get to a donation centre in regional areas, Lifeblood is urging those able to book in an appointment now.

Lifeblood donor experience director Cath Stone said while donations surged after the Bondi attack, appointments had dipped in the lead-up to Easter.

“We saw an extraordinary response from people across Australia in December, with record numbers of donations from the community during a time of need,” she said.

“The need for blood continues well beyond any single event. Blood is needed every day, and demand can spike around Easter due to increased road accidents and ongoing cancer treatment needs, at a time when many regular donors take a break.

“Right now, we need more type O and A donors, and we know 23,000 of the first-time donors who gave after the attack were type O or A donors.”

Plasma donations are also in demand, accounting for around half of all donations needed this Easter long weekend.

Donations are especially needed on Good Friday, with many donor centres opening nationwide and operating across the entire long weekend.

To book a donation visit lifeblood.com.au, use the Lifeblood app or call 13 14 95.

title: Perth Corporate Rumble XIV: White collar boxing exhibition raises money for a good cause
plaintext:
Perth’s white collar workers donned the boxing gloves and helmets for a good cause on Saturday night as Perth Corporate Rumble XIV brought the heat to Crown Towers.

A crowd of 1200 were treated to a premium three course dinner and beverage experience as competitors — who had undergone intense training regimens for their scheduled bouts — traded blows in the ring.

With non-stop action throughout the night, 15 high intensity bouts took place, with celebrations continuing at the Merrywell for those looking to go the distance.

Well-dressed guests included business owners, influencers, and local corporates.

Founded in 2016 by Glen Austin and Nigel Groves, the annual boxing exhibition raises money for the Saba Rose Button Foundation — a non-profit helping to support children with additional needs and their families.

title: Illinois knocks off Iowa to reach Final Four after buzzer malfunction delay
plaintext:
For the first time in more than two decades, the Illinois men's basketball team will still be dancing when the Final Four tips off.

Iowa’s underdog run in the NCAA Tournament ended Saturday with a 71-59 loss to a dominant Illinois team. Before Illinois could cut down the nets at Houston’s Toyota Center, a buzzer malfunction caused a loud, roughly 10-minute delay.

The buzzer initially sounded signaling the end of a media timeout with just under eight minutes remaining in the first half. The horn continued blaring for about another seven minutes.

Players stood on the court ready to play for a couple of minutes before both teams started to warm up as the buzzer continued to sound.

It was finally silenced, to cheers from the crowd, but then the main scoreboard and video screen that hangs over the middle of the court went dark.

The game ultimately resumed with the big scoreboard still off. Two smaller scoreboards at each end of the arena were working.

Freshman guard Keaton Wagler scored 25 points to help secure Illinois' first Final Four berth since 2005.

This will be the sixth overall trip to the Final Four for Illinois, which has never won a national title. The Fighting Illini will face either Duke or UConn next week in Indianapolis.

title: President Aoun condemns attack on UNIFIL, offers condolences over fallen peacekeeper
plaintext:
President Joseph Aoun held a phone call on Monday with UNIFIL’s Head of Mission and Force Commander, Diodato Abagnara, to offer condolences following the killing of an Indonesian peacekeeper.

The soldier was killed after his post in the town of Aadchit el-Qsair came under shelling, which also left another peacekeeper wounded.

Aoun reiterated his condemnation of attacks targeting peacekeeping forces operating in southern Lebanon, wishing the injured soldier a swift recovery. He also praised the sacrifices made by international troops serving within the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon in the south.

title: Israeli media reports Haifa oil refinery hit after attack
plaintext:
Israeli media reported that an oil refinery in the northern city of Haifa had been hit on Monday, shortly after the military said it had detected new incoming missiles from Iran.

Television network Channel 12 showed thick black smoke billowing into the sky from the site, while Israel's Fire and Rescue Services shared photos of a cylinder-shaped tank on fire.

It was not immediately clear whether the refinery had been directly hit by a missile from Iran or Lebanon, or by debris from an interception.

Footage acquired by AFP, filmed shortly after media reported the impact, showed a plume of dark smoke rising above cylindrical tanks.

AFP journalists who arrived at the scene around an hour later said the smoke had dissipated and firefighters were working to manage the fire.

The refinery in Haifa, Israel's third-largest city, is located in a large industrial zone.

"Search and rescue forces, both reserve and regular forces, are on their way to a site in northern Israel where reports of impact have been received," Israel's military said in a statement, without specifying the precise location.

Israeli media said the site was also hit on March 19 when Iran fired missiles towards Israel.

title: Vukic brought down as Moroccans make their mark
plaintext:
Australia's Aleksandar Vukic has found himself on the wrong end of a little piece of history after making a first-round exit at the Grand Prix Hassan II in Marrakech.

His 6-2 3-6 6-1 defeat by world No.587 Taa Baadi provided Morocco with their first ATP Tour match winner in eight years.

Vukic, 29, who reached a world ranking high of 48 in 2023, is currently ranked 84 and had a run to the quarter-finals of the Adelaide International in January.

The home crowd's delight was soon doubled when another Moroccan wildcard, world No.731 Karim Bennani, saw off France's Quentin Halys 6-4 6-7 (5-7) 6-2 in the next match on centre court.

Bennani, who at 18 became the youngest Moroccan to win an ATP match, said: "I don't know what to say. I still can't believe what just happened. Thank you to the amazing Moroccan crowd, my coaches, my parents…I'm just really happy with the win."

title: Rollover crash with vehicle carrying infants leaves 1 person dead in Riverside
plaintext:
A vehicle carrying two infants was involved in a fatal rollover crash in riverside Sunday night, according to media reports.

The crash occurred on the northbound 215 freeway near Eucalyptus Avenue around 7:30 p.m., KABC reported.

The circumstances of what caused the crash were not immediately available, but two adults and two infants were left trapped in the wreck, NBC4 reported. One person died but it was not immediately clear if they were an adult or child, the station reported.

The Riverside Fire Department responded to the scene and took multiple people to the hospital, including one minor to a trauma center, according to KCBS.

First-responders used the jaws of life to free the vehicle’s passengers, according to media reports.

title: Twelve cars damaged in protest against ‘coronation of Nigerian king’ in KuGompo City
plaintext:
Eastern Cape police have confirmed 12 cars were damaged on Monday during a traditional leaders’ protest against the reported coronation of a Nigerian “king” in KuGompo City.

Police spokesperson Brig Nobuntu Gantana confirmed that police were at the scene “of a protest that turned violent”.

“The protest began as a planned peaceful demonstration by various traditional leaders over the alleged coronation of an Igbo king,” Gantana said.

“Violence broke out when some participants moved away from the city hall, where a petition was being handed over.

“Twelve vehicles have been damaged, several buildings have been torched, and no injuries have been reported.”

Emergency services, including the fire department, remain on the scene, along with the public order policing unit.

“This is an active scene, and updates and information will be shared [later],” Gantana said.

This is a developing story.

Daily Dispatch

title: IGO takes hunt for red metal Stateside with $6m Buxton Resources buyout
plaintext:
Battery metals miner IGO has broadened its copper pursuit to the US after paying $6 million for a slice of what it believes is shaping up as a prospective district for the red metal.

The Ivan Vella-led miner will part with $6.15 million in cash to secure the remaining 49 per cent of a joint venture with listed junior Buxton Resources, according to an ASX filing on Monday. The deal will hand IGO 100 per cent ownership of the central Copper Wolf exploration project in the US state of Arizona.

Copper Wolf is IGO’s only exploration prospect outside of Australia, with the deal in line with the company’s focus to find more of the red metal. Several years of low lithium and nickel prices, which have recently shown some signs of revival in recent months, prompted the company to channel more of a focus on better-faring commodities.

Copper prices rose significantly through 2025 and hit an all-time high in January amid huge frenzy surrounding its potential use in electrification and data centres.

“Consolidating full ownership of Copper Wolf is a logical and disciplined step aligned with IGO’s growth strategy to discover, develop and deliver battery minerals, including copper,” Mr Vella said.

“The transaction simplifies the asset structure and provides IGO with direct exposure to an area with highly prospective geological potential in the globally significant Arizona copper belt.”

Shares in IGO closed the day 2 per cent higher to $8.09 on the news.

title: Easter recipe: Perth chef Guillaume Groult’s perfect seafood sauce  beurre nantais (beurre blanc)
plaintext:
Even the freshest catch can benefit from the right sauce to quietly transform it from simple to sublime.

Enter beurre nantais (or beurre blanc), the classic French accompaniment that’s perfect for serving with a wide range of seafood.

Perth chef Guillaume Groult, executive chef at The Heritage in the city, says beurre nantais is a velvety, emulsified butter sauce.

“At The Heritage we serve this versatile sauce with snapper and mussels or a nice piece of barbecued swordfish but it will also be delicious with scallops or any seafood,” he says.

Here’s how to recreate it at home this Easter.

Makes 3-4 serves

2 shallots, very finely minced

100ml dry white wine

40ml white wine vinegar

50ml thick cream

150g cold unsalted butter, diced

salt

Combine shallots, wine, and vinegar in a saucepan.

Reduce on medium heat until almost dry (about 2-3 tbsp of liquid left).

Add the cream and reduce again slightly until lightly thickened.Lower the heat and whisk in the cold butter cube by cube to emulsify, then season.

Once the sauce is prepared, keep it warm but don’t boil it again.

Tip: If the sauce splits, whisk it again with a little bit of cold water or cold cream.
sentence: Lower the heat and whisk in the cold butter cube by cube to emulsify, then season.
context:
Even the freshest catch can benefit from the right sauce to quietly transform it from simple to sublime.

Enter beurre nantais (or beurre blanc), the classic French accompaniment that’s perfect for serving with a wide range of seafood.

Perth chef Guillaume Groult, executive chef at The Heritage in the city, says beurre nantais is a velvety, emulsified butter sauce.

“At The Heritage we serve this versatile sauce with snapper and mussels or a nice piece of barbecued swordfish but it will also be delicious with scallops or any seafood,” he says.

Here’s how to recreate it at home this Easter.

Makes 3-4 serves

2 shallots, very finely minced

100ml dry white wine

40ml white wine vinegar

50ml thick cream

150g cold unsalted butter, diced

salt

Combine shallots, wine, and vinegar in a saucepan.

Reduce on medium heat until almost dry (about 2-3 tbsp of liquid left).

Add the cream and reduce again slightly until lightly thickened.LTHA whisk in the cold butter cube by cube to emulsify, then season.

Once the sauce is prepared, keep it warm but don’t boil it again.

Tip: If the sauce splits, whisk it again with a little bit of cold water or cold cream.

title: Gosnells: Detectives probe terrifying hit-and-run after man struck by 4WD during late night walk with friend
plaintext:
Detectives are probing a terrifying hit-and-run after a man was struck by a 4WD during a late night walk in Perth’s south east.

Police say the 36-year-old was walking with a friend along a footpath near Corfield and Verna Streets, in Gosnells, when a white 4WD wagon — fitted with bull bars — mounted the footpath and smashed into him about 9.30pm on Sunday.

“The impact caused the victim to collide with a Colourbond fence on the corner of Corfield and Verna Streets,” a police spokesperson said.

He was rushed to Fiona Stanley Hospital for medical treatment.

The vehicle did not stop and fled the scene, with detectives continuing their hunt for the driver on Monday morning.

Detectives urge anyone who saw or has vision of the crash, to contact Crime Stoppers online at www.crimestopperswa.com.au or by calling 1800 333 000.

title: Passenger bomb threat triggers police response and evacuation on Frontier flight under investigation
plaintext:
A Frontier Airlines flight was moved to an isolated runway at Atlanta’s airport Sunday after a passenger made a bomb threat, prompting a law enforcement response before officials deemed the threat non-credible.

Frontier Flight 2539 from Columbus, Ohio, had landed at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport and was taxiing to the gate when a passenger allegedly made the verbal threat.

As a precaution and in coordination with local authorities, the aircraft was redirected to a remote location where law enforcement responded.

Passengers deplaned using airstairs and were bused to the terminal. Authorities later determined the threat was not credible.

The FBI and Atlanta Police Department told Fox News the incident remains under investigation, with Atlanta police taking the lead.

Atlanta police would not say whether anyone has been arrested.
sentence: A Frontier Airlines flight was moved to an isolated runway at Atlanta’s airport Sunday after a passenger made a bomb threat, prompting a law enforcement response before officials deemed the threat non-credible.

title: Sinner joins Sabalenka with 'Sunshine Double' glory
plaintext:
Jannik Sinner has rolled to a 6-4 6-4 victory over Jiri Lehecka to capture the Miami Open title and complete the "Sunshine Double'', a day after Aryna Sabalenka accomplished the same feat in a win over Coco Gauff.

The last male player to complete the double - winning Indian Wells followed by Miami in the same season - was Roger Federer in 2017.

But Sinner is the first male to do so without dropping a set in either Masters 1000 tournament.

The second-seeded Sinner didn't let either of the two 90-minute rain delays slow him on Sunday. He notched 10 aces in the final, and 70 in the tournament for the second-most of his career.

He often had world No.21 Lehecka running from side to side with his precise, deep groundstrokes.

Sinner, 24, won his first 23 first-service points — a streak that lasted midway into the second set.

He became the eighth male to run the table at Indian Wells and Miami, and he and Sabalenka became the first to accomplish the double in the same year since Novak Djokovic and Victoria Azarenka in 2016.

This was Sinner's second Miami Open title in three years as he ran his Hard Rock Stadium win streak to 12 matches dating to 2024. Last year, he missed the tournament serving a suspension for testing positive for a banned substance.

Lehecka, 24, in his first ATP 1000 final, was a big underdog, losing all three prior matches to Sinner.

title: Two Blues stun favourites to claim Eastern Goldfields Cricket Association B-grade flag
plaintext:
Great Boulder captain Nathan Weir grabbed five wickets and teammate Kyle Penny blasted an unbeaten 46 in the side’s eight-wicket rout of North Kalgoorlie on Sunday in the Eastern Goldfields Cricket Association’s B-grade grand final.

Norths went into the clash at Wallace Park as favourites after claiming the minor premiership, but they were bowled out for 56 in 27.2 overs when Weir led the attack with 5-17.

It was Norths’ lowest score of the season, with Weir’s haul complemented by Penny and Nathan Lovell, who finished with 2-16 and 2-6, respectively.

Great Boulder, who had finished third after the qualifiers, immediately had Norths on the ropes at 4-6 with Matt Taylor (15 off 37 balls) and Kodee Lowe (13 off 16 balls) the top batters.

In reply, Great Boulder initially stuttered to 2-28, but Penny took on the opposition bowlers that yielded six boundaries and two sixes as the side wrapped-up victory in 11.1 overs.

Penny’s effort with the blade capped-off a big season when he averaged 40.50 and Weir’s match-winning haul was the icing on the cake for an overall competition-best 23 wickets at 12.87.
sentence: Great Boulder captain Nathan Weir grabbed five wickets and teammate Kyle Penny blasted an unbeaten 46 in the side’s eight-wicket rout of North Kalgoorlie on Sunday in the Eastern Goldfields Cricket Association’s B-grade grand final.

title: Letters to the Editor: Judging Disney adults? Maybe you’ve just lost your own inner child
plaintext:
To the editor: It was interesting reading the philosophical perspective about adults who love Disney (“We shouldn’t treat Disney adults like cultural abominations,” March 26). But from a psychological point of view, I think most adults have lost the child in them, and they think adults who have kept that part of themselves have just never grown up.

Imagination is one of the most important parts of the mind that we take for granted, and it has very little to do with maturity. How many of us have recently gotten into a pillow fight or rolled down a grassy hill?

Keeping your inner child increases your enjoyment of life. Oh, and I still love going on the “Alice in Wonderland” ride.

Bruce Halpern, Torrance
sentence: Imagination is one of the most important parts of the mind that we take for granted, and it has very little to do with maturity.

title: Bafana and Panama need to upgrade the level before the World Cup, says Panama coach
plaintext:
Panama coach Thomas Christiansen has called for his team and Bafana Bafana to step up a gear before the World Cup to be co-hosted by the US, Mexico and Canada this winter.

Bafana and Panama played to a 1-1 draw in a friendly at Moses Mabhida Stadium on Friday. Oswin Appollis and Édgar Bárcenas found the back of the net for their respective teams. The two sides meet again in another friendly as they continue to prepare for the global showpiece, at Cape Town Stadium on Tuesday (7.30pm).

“I saw what I expected from this team. They are a physical team, fast in attack, good in transition, good at runs, and they exploited the spaces we wanted to close, but a friendly game or a qualifying game is not the same as a game in the World Cup, so I think we both need to upgrade our level a bit,” Christiansen said when asked what he makes of Bafana.

The Danish coach also explained why they chose to play against Bafana. Panama are pitted against Ghana, England and Croatia in Group L in the upcoming World Cup.

“The goal was to play against an African team,” said Christiansen.

“There are constant similarities with Ghana to what we will find with South Africa, and that was the objective. By playing two games against South Africa, we can change our ideas, positions, and players, and that’s what we want.

“It should also provide us the opportunity to rotate the squad a bit and give minutes to those who perhaps haven’t seen any playing time with the national team.”

title: MLB top prospect shares hilarious moment with veteran who robbed his home run after four-hit MLB debut
plaintext:
Detroit Tigers top prospect Kevin McGonigle seems pretty comfortable at the major league level, but one veteran doesn't want him too comfortable.

McGonigle made the Tigers' Opening Day roster, and he showed why he deserved it by notching four hits in his MLB debut.

McGonigle was back in action on Friday in San Diego against the Padres, and in the top of the second inning, he launched a ball to deep center field that traveled 403 feet. However, Jackson Merrill leaped and robbed McGonigle of his first home run.

In the bottom half of the inning, after Merrill doubled, McGonigle appeared to compliment Merrill on his catch, which Merrill thanked him for.

But that did not come before Merrill joked about McGonigle's big Opening Day.

"You got four yesterday!" Merrill said to the rookie.

McGonigle became the 21st player in MLB history to have four hits in his MLB debut and the first to do it since J.P. Arancibia in 2010.

Merrill's home run robbery provided a boost early on, but McGonigle got revenge with a game-winning two-run single in a four-run eighth inning for Detroit en route to a 5-2 victory.

The Tigers choked a double-digit AL Central lead last season but still managed to get to the American League Division Series after defeating the same team who overtook them in the standings in the Cleveland Guardians.

The Padres played more October baseball as a wild-card team as the Los Angeles Dodgers took another NL West title before repeating as World Series Champions.

title: Russia declares flooding emergency in Dagestan capital
plaintext:
Authorities in the capital of the southern Russian region of Dagestan have declared a state of emergency after heavy rainfall led to widespread flooding and power cuts in the region.

"Emergency services have been placed on high alert, efforts are under way to deal with the aftermath, and assistance will be provided to affected residents," the Makhachkala city administration said on Telegram.

More than 327,000 people remain without power in Dagestan due to bad weather, the regional ministry of emergency situations said.

"As of 12:00, 283 settlements with a population of 327,183 people, including 89,705 children, remain without electricity," the ministry said on its website.

The region's head Sergei Melikov said that the emergency services and officials had been preparing for worsening weather conditions but the actual situation "exceeded even the most pessimistic forecasts".

Officials in Dagestan's second-largest city of Khasavyurt said a railway bridge had been damaged due to heavy rainfall.

"Two spans of the bridge collapsed on the Khasavyurt–Kadiyurt section of the North Caucasus Railway," Dagestan's government press service said.

The heavy rain in the region is set to continue until Sunday, according to forecasts.

title: SA claim key wicket after more rain in Shield final
plaintext:
South Australia claimed the key wicket of Victorian young gun Oliver Peake after rain further disrupted the Sheffield Shield final.

Marcus Harris and Fergus O'Neill then survived to lunch on Saturday at Junction Oval, leaving the home side 5-150 in an evenly poised game.

SA made 198 in their first innings, just three short of a crucial batting bonus point. That means they must win the final to win back-to-back Shield titles for the first time.

If the final is drawn, Victoria will win the Shield as the top-ranked team.

Play was delayed for more than an hour on day three because of persistent rain, after interruptions on Thursday and Friday.

But the five-day game and the low scores so far mean a result is likely.

Bowling around the wicket and wide of the stumps, Nathan McAndrew dismissed Peake for 28 with a superb delivery that hit the top of off stump.

It left the Victorians in strife at 5-127, but Harris and O'Neill held firm as SA tried to build on their counter-punch.

Harris had made 40 from 121 balls and O'Neill was on 11.

Victoria scored 1-40 from 16.3 overs in Saturday's morning session.

The weather had cleared by early afternoon and conditions are expected to hold for the rest of the day.

But the five-day game and the low scores so far mean a result is likely.

Bowling around the wicket and wide of the stumps, Nathan McAndrew dismissed Peake for 28 with a superb delivery that hit the top of off stump.

It left the Victorians in strife at 5-127, but Harris and O'Neill held firm as SA tried to build on their counter-punch.

Harris had made 40 from 121 balls and O'Neill was on 11.

Victoria scored 1-40 from 16.3 overs in Saturday's morning session.

The weather had cleared by early afternoon and conditions are expected to hold for the rest of the day.

The weather had cleared by early afternoon and conditions are expected to hold for the rest of the day.

title: Site chosen for tail of crashed Boeing 737 water bomber
plaintext:
The tail of a Boeing 737 water bomber that crashed while fighting a fire near Hopetoun in 2023 is set to become a roadside attraction.

The blackened tail — which measures 14 metres wide and 11 metres high — will be displayed at the intersection of Hopetoun-Ravensthorpe and Jerdacuttup roads.

The aircraft crashed in Fitzgerald River National Park in February 2023 — but, thankfully, the two pilots on board survived unscathed.

The incident was the first loss of a 737 aircraft on Australian soil.

The Shire of Ravensthorpe was gifted the tail after the crash and agreed to work with the Mt Short Hopetoun Aero Club on how to display the relic.

Councillors at last week’s council meeting voted to display the tail at the intersection on shire-owned farmland in a project estimated to cost $80,000.

The meeting agenda item report said costs of the project — including land clearing, fencing, and installation — would be shared by the shire and the aero club.

title: Trump signs executive order ensuring TSA workers are paid during DHS shutdown
plaintext:
President Donald Trump on Friday signed an executive order directing federal officials to ensure Transportation Security Administration (TSA) employees receive back pay during the ongoing Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown.

Trump described the situation as an "emergency," citing severe strain on airport security operations.

"Accordingly, I hereby direct the Secretary of Homeland Security, in coordination with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, to use funds that have a reasonable and logical nexus to TSA operations to provide TSA employees with the compensation and benefits that would have accrued to them if not for the Democrat-led DHS shutdown, consistent with applicable law, including 31 U.S.C. 1301(a)," Trump said.
sentence: "Accordingly, I hereby direct the Secretary of Homeland Security, in coordination with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, to use funds that have a reasonable and logical nexus to TSA operations to provide TSA employees with the compensation and benefits that would have accrued to them if not for the Democrat-led DHS shutdown, consistent with applicable law, including 31 U.S.C. 1301(a)," Trump said.

title: Pupil dies after wall collapse at Daveyton school
plaintext:
A Grade 3 pupil from Lerutle Primary School in Daveyton, Ekurhuleni, has died after a section of a wall collapsed during breaktime on Thursday.

The incident happened while pupils were outside during their break. Part of the wall suddenly fell, trapping six pupils underneath.

The injured pupils were taken to different hospitals for urgent treatment. One of the pupils later died from his injuries.

Gauteng department of education MEC Matome Chiloane said the department would make sure support is given to those affected.

“We are deeply saddened by this tragic incident and the loss of such a young life. Our thoughts and prayers are with the pupil’s family, fellow learners, educators and the entire school community as they mourn this devastating loss,” Chiloane said.

“The circumstances around the wall collapse are still under investigation,” he said.

title: SA racer Jonathan Mogotsi earns ‘Ring Licence’ for Nürburgring 24 Hours
plaintext:
Volkswagen Motorsport driver Jonathan Mogotsi has secured his Nürburgring Permit A licence, clearing the way for him to compete in this year’s Nürburgring 24 Hours on May 18 to 19.

The permit, often referred to as a “Ring Licence”, is a requirement for drivers competing on the demanding Nürburgring Nordschleife. Mogotsi completed the qualification during the second round of the Nürburgring Langstrecken-Serie (NLS) on March 21, after the season-opening race was cancelled due to snow.

“It’s job done,” said Mogotsi after the race. “I’ve earned my Nürburgring Permit A, which allows me to compete in the 24-hour race in May. It’s been an eye-opener and I’ve learned a lot over the past few weeks.”

He noted that the real-world circuit presents a far greater challenge than expected. “Video games come nowhere near showing how narrow, bumpy and blind the corners are,” he said.

Mogotsi will return to Germany for the next NLS round on April 11 as part of his final preparations for the endurance race.

The South African driver is competing with Max Kruse Racing, which fields three Volkswagen Golf 8 TCR cars in both the NLS series and the Nürburgring 24 Hours.

title: Kremlin says Russia not seeking to undermine US business in CPC oil group
plaintext:
Russia is not seeking to undermine U.S. business interests in the Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC), Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Friday.

He was responding to a question about comments by several Russian experts, including on state TV, that Russia should ban oil supplies via the CPC from Kazakhstan in response to Washington's sanctions against Russia.

CPC, in which U.S. oil majors Chevron CVX.N and ExxonMobil XOM.N are large shareholders, exports its oil from a Russian terminal on the Black Sea.

"In addition to American partners, there are also our Kazakh partners involved, and Russia remains a reliable guarantor of global energy security, despite everything," Peskov told a daily conference call with reporters.

CPC, whose terminal is located near Russia's Black Sea port of Novorossiysk, was targeted by Ukrainian drones and has had to scale back its operations due to the attacks.

Peskov accused Ukraine of "energy blackmail" over the strikes, which he said were causing Russian, U.S. and Kazakh companies to suffer.

(Reporting by Dmitry Antonov. Writing by Vladimir Soldatkin. Editing by Mark Trevelyan and Mark Potter)

title: Moment driver arrested hiding in Melbourne shopping centre dumpster after alleged police chase across city suburbs
plaintext:
A driver has been caught hiding in a dumpster after allegedly fleeing from police across multiple suburbs in Melbourne’s southeast.

Footage captured by the Victoria Police Air Wing reveals the moment officers zero in on the man’s position, as he squats in a large rubbish bin in the loading bay of Carrum Downs shopping centre.

When busted by police, the 40-year-old man - dressed in an orange hi-vis hoodie - can be seen climbing out of the dumpster before lying on the ground and being taken into custody.

Earlier, police were alerted to a Holden commodore allegedly “travelling erratically” in Mount Eliza, an hour southeast of Melbourne, about 10am on Friday.

Officers attempted to pull the vehicle over on Gulls Way in Frankston South, but he allegedly fled from police.

The police Air Wing tracked the commodore driving into the Carrum Downs shopping centre on Halls Road, where police used a mix of ground patrols, eyes in the sky and security cameras to home in on the man’s location.

“The Dandenong man was taken into custody and has been interviewed in relation to his driving behaviour,” a Victoria Police spokeswoman said.

“His commodore was also impounded.”

The investigation into the incident is ongoing.

title: Former foster carer accused of historical sexual abuse
plaintext:
A former foster carer is set to face court accused of a string of historical sexual abuse allegations against five victims.

South Australian police on Thursday arrested a 56-year-old man from Adelaide’s southern suburbs who they say is linked to a host of sexual abuse allegations.

The allegations relate to incidents said to have occurred between 2018 and 2025 – as well as further accusations between 1998 and 2010 – when the man was a foster carer.

The offences are alleged to have been committed in South Australia’s Mid North and in the metropolitan Adelaide areas.

It is alleged the offences were carried out against five individuals, with charges laid against the man on Thursday.

Following his arrest, he was refused bail and is due to appear at Adelaide Magistrates Court at the earliest available opportunity, likely to be on Friday.

title: EU says Russia gave Iran intelligence to kill Americans in Mideast
plaintext:
The EU's top diplomat, on Thursday, accused Russia of providing intelligence support to Iran in the Middle East war to "kill Americans," calling on the United States to increase pressure on Moscow.

"We see that Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans, and Russia is also supporting Iran now with the drones so that they can attack neighbouring countries and also U.S. military bases," Kaja Kallas told journalists at a G7 meeting outside Paris.

"These wars are very much interlinked... If America wants the war in the Middle East to stop, Iran to stop attacking them, they should also put the pressure on Russia so that they are not able to help them in this," she added, referring to Moscow's invasion of Ukraine.

title: House That! Ashfield facing new price record at stunning home, where riverfront living can be taken literally- because there is not even a road between the house and the water
plaintext:
A double-storey riverfront house for sale in Ashfield is seeking to set a new price record for the suburb, listing this week for offers from $2.3 million.

The rare beauty is riverfront in the most literal sense, with not even a road between the home and the water.

Set at an elbow of the river, the four-bedroom, two-bathroom home on a 1538sqm block has views up two riverbeds and to Ron Courtney Island.

With a vast expanse of bushland to the east, and barely a neighbour in sight, it’s difficult to believe it’s only 10km from the CBD.

The sales price is set to exceed the $2.02m paid for a three-bedroom home on a 500sqm block a year ago, and is about three times the median value of $810,000, according to REIWA.

The home is being sold by owners David and Lynette Love, who have marvelled at the “amazing and quiet” setting for 23 years. Mr Love said the “unrestricted, forever views” and the aspect were best features.

The home’s expansive balcony stretches nearly the width of the home, while big windows draw in natural light.

The upper level has the living, dining, kitchen and master bedroom — all designed to look over the river — as well as two more bedrooms, two bathrooms and an office. The lower level has a theatre, a bedroom, bathroom, workshop, study and storage.

It is being sold by property agent Kelly Jones.

title: Shire of Ravensthorpe gets its own official flag as ‘symbol of local pride’
plaintext:
The Shire of Ravensthorpe will soon have its own official flag, displaying the shire logo on a plain background of either blue, green or white.

Councillors at Saturday’s ordinary council meeting unanimously voted to adopt a flag to act as “a symbol of local pride, unity and heritage”.

The meeting agenda item report said the flag could “foster community identity, represent its unique regional history (such as mining, agriculture and wildflowers), and distinguish itself as a distinct civic entity”.

It said the flag could be used for official events, branding and promoting tourism in the townships of Ravensthorpe and Hopetoun.

The shire’s logo is a circle split into three sections with various symbols of mining and farming, including wheat and a poppet head.

The report said the use of the logo as the design would connect the shire’s visual identity or brand to the flag, and that the logo embodies the region’s diverse identity “blending natural beauty with industry”.

Flag poles will be installed outside each of the shire offices in Ravensthorpe and Hopetoun.

It will be produced in standard flag proportions “for maximum visibility”.

title: Israeli army says 91st Division expanding operations in South Lebanon
plaintext:
The Israeli army said its 91st Division is continuing ground operations in South Lebanon, targeting what it described as infrastructure and military capabilities belonging to Hezbollah.

In a post on X, Israeli army spokesperson Avichay Adraee said the division is conducting focused ground operations to expand what he described as a forward security zone in the area.

Adraee claimed that, so far, Israeli forces have destroyed more than 350 Hezbollah targets and killed more than 330 fighters, including dozens from the Radwan Force.

He added that troops have also seized various military equipment, including night-vision devices, protective vests, launchers, RPG rounds, ammunition magazines, Kalashnikov rifles, and machine guns.

title: ‘She’s finally here’: Former Miss SA Natasha Joubert welcomes first baby
plaintext:
Joubert and her husband, Enrico Vermaak, announced their pregnancy in October last year.

Former Miss South Africa, Natasha Joubert, has welcomed her first child.

Joubert shared the news on Instagram on Wednesday.

She revealed her daughter, Klein Vermaakie, was born on 11 March.

“At 41 weeks and two days, after 30 hours of labour, she’s finally here. Our greatest, biggest blessing -Klein Vermaakie,” she wrote.

Joubert and her husband, Enrico Vermaak, announced their pregnancy in October last year.

They hosted a baby shower at Rosemary Hill Farm in November.

The event featured pink décor, including blush, dusty rose and pastel tones.

“Our dearest baby girl. Celebrating you. We could not have dreamt of a more beautiful and perfect day,” Joubert wrote in a caption on Instagram.

Pregnancy journey

Joubert previously spoke about challenges during her pregnancy.

“I have been challenged mentally, physically and psychologically. I’ve had many turbulences with friendships and work and have felt guilty, not just feeling joyous,” she said.

She said she took time to process her emotions before sharing the news publicly.

“I wanted to ensure I work through a lot so that now I can fully embrace and feel all the amazing emotions. Enrico and I are on cloud 10 000 000. We love this little one so much.”

title: POLL | Does the move to charge Masemola and other senior officers give you hope SAPS is being cleaned up?
plaintext:
The massive corruption scandal involving a R360m health services tender has raised serious questions about the integrity of the South African Police Service (SAPS).

The controversial contract, awarded to tenderpreneur Vusimuzi “Cat” Matlala’s company Medicare24 Tshwane District in 2024, was cancelled after investigations uncovered irregularities.

On Tuesday the Investigating Directorate Against Corruption (Idac) arrested several high-ranking SAPS officials. They face charges including fraud and corruption, money laundering and violations of the Public Finance Management Act (PFMA).

The officials appeared in the Pretoria magistrate’s court, where they were granted bail.

National police commissioner Gen Fannie Masemola was served with a summons related to the tender. He is scheduled to appear in court on April 21. The Presidency has confirmed President Cyril Ramaphosa was formally notified of the National Prosecuting Authority’s (NPA) decision to proceed.

The DA has called for Masemola’s precautionary suspension, citing the severity of his alleged links to the fraudulent tender.

Despite concerns about the level of corruption, analysts view the developments as a landmark moment for South Africa’s criminal justice system. The move suggests a growing willingness within law enforcement to hold even the “top cop” accountable, signalling no-one is above the law.

title: Former Fremantle Docker Will Brodie to play first AFL game in more than two years after Port Adelaide switch
plaintext:
For the first time since 2023, Will Brodie will be back in the AFL with the former Docker set for his Port Adelaide debut against West Coast on Sunday.

Brodie was traded to the Power for pick No.103 last year, despite having one year left on his contract, having spent his final two and a half years at Fremantle stuck behind their lauded midfield brigade.

Despite turning in eye catching perfomances for WAFL affiliate Peel, including being part of their 2024 premiership triumph, Brodie was unable to force his way back into the Dockers plans.

The 27-year-old requested the move, which saw him reunite with former Fremantle midfield turned Port Adelaide coach Josh Carr.

“It was a pretty tough midfield to get into a Freo, there are some studs in there as there is so, so I’m just going to keep trying to work, and I think I can get back to my best,” he said late last year.

“I was at the point where I was really craving some more opportunity, and I jumped at the chance for a fresh start.

“It’s been a tough journey the last few years but I’m excited and motivated to give it another go.”

Brodie was overlooked for the first two rounds of the season, but injuries to Connor Rozee and Miles Bergman left the door open for his return.

He was impressive in the final pre-season SANFL hit-out with three goals leading them to a tight win over West Adelaide.

For the first time since 2023, Will Brodie will be back in the AFL with the former Docker set for his Port Adelaide debut against West Coast on Sunday.

Brodie was traded to the Power for pick No.103 last year, despite having one year left on his contract, having spent his final two and a half years at Fremantle stuck behind their lauded midfield brigade.

Despite turning in eye catching perfomances for WAFL affiliate Peel, including being part of their 2024 premiership triumph, Brodie was unable to force his way back into the Dockers plans.

The 27-year-old requested the move, which saw him reunite with former Fremantle midfield turned Port Adelaide coach Josh Carr.

“It was a pretty tough midfield to get into a Freo, there are some studs in there as there is so, so I’m just going to keep trying to work, and I think I can get back to my best,” he said late last year.

“I was at the point where I was really craving some more opportunity, and I jumped at the chance for a fresh start.

“It’s been a tough journey the last few years but I’m excited and motivated to give it another go.”

Brodie was overlooked FTFT rounds of the season, but injuries to Connor Rozee and Miles Bergman left the door open for his return.

He was impressive in the final pre-season SANFL hit-out with three goals leading them to a tight win over West Adelaide.

title: Family’s Australia roadtrip ruined as high-tech thieves use legal key-cloning gadget to steal, burn out 4WD
plaintext:
A concerning new detail is emerging in car thefts, after a group of thieves managed to swipe an Australian family’s 4WD using cloned car keys.

Hayley Kent, her husband and their four children were caravanning across the nation, when the thieves struck using a key fob generator.

The generator works by hacking into the car’s computer, wiping the existing keys and creating new ones.

It is legal to buy and can easily purchased online.

“My husband got home about 5.30pm and said ‘where’s the car,’” Ms Kent told 7News.

“It’s just a kick in the guts.”

The family’s LandCruiser was found gutted this week, burnt out with heavy damage.

“I am going back to the old steering wheel lock and having everything on it,” she said.

NRMA Insurance’s Shawn Ticehurst told 7News keyless car thefts have become a “global trend”.

Keeping your car keys stored in a faraday punch or even wrapped in aluminium foil so that it cannot receive signals and be wiped are easy ways to thwart potential car thieves.

Keeping your car keys stored in a faraday punch or even wrapped in aluminium foil so that it cannot receive signals and be wiped are easy ways to thwart potential car thieves.

title: Gloucester Tree reopening pushed back to June after 30 months of waiting
plaintext:
The climb up Pemberton’s iconic Gloucester Tree has again been delayed, with authorities now aiming for a mid-year reopening.

The Department of Biodiversity, Conservation and Attractions confirmed the setback this week, with a DBCA spokesperson saying the work to re-establish the climb and viewing platform has taken longer than anticipated.

The Gloucester Tree was first closed to climbing in 2023 when an engineering assessment identified structural issues with the platform at the top of the tree.

At the time the DBCA said it hoped to have people climbing again by the end of 2024, but now, 30 months later an opening date was yet to be set.

“Installing new infrastructure into a living tree is a highly complex task, and ensuring the final experience is safe for visitors remains DBCA’s highest priority,” the spokesperson said.

“The arborist advising DBCA on the project has recommended some revisions and these additional works, along with some refinements to the surface finishes of built components, have extended the project timeline.

“DBCA is now targeting a June 2026 completion, however, the complexity of installation may impact this date.

“DBCA appreciates the public’s patience and looks forward to welcoming visitors back to the Gloucester Tree soon.”
sentence: “DBCA is now targeting a June 2026 completion, however, the complexity of installation may impact this date.
