title: Australia, Germany eye military deployment pact
plaintext:
Australia and Germany are working to “make it much easier” for defence personnel from both countries to be stationed in each other’s territory, Defence Minister Richard Marles has announced.

Mr Marles and his German counterpart held talks in Canberra on Thursday to discuss deeper defence co-operation amid the war raging in Ukraine and simmering tensions in the Indo-Pacific.

“We share values. We are democracies. We respect freedom of speech. We respect the rule of law at home and internationally,” Mr Marles told reporters at a post-meet press conference.

“The rules-based order is deeply important to both of our countries and as such, to be able to work closely with Germany is critical in what is a very challenging world.

“And … today there is a deep sense of connection between the Indo-Pacific and Europe.

“The moment we saw a no-limits agreement signed between Russia and China on the eve of the war in Ukraine really was the moment that a war in Eastern Europe became critically important to Australia.”

The announcement comes after Anthony Albanese and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a defence partnership between Australia and the EU.

More to come

title: South West the home of French films as Alliance Francaise French Film Festival kicks off in Bunbury
plaintext:
The South West is the home of French films this week, with the Alliance Francaise French Film Festival kicking off in Bunbury on Wednesday night.

The event started with a with a screening of Cedric Klapisch’s Colours of Time (La Venue De L’Avenir) at the Bunbury Regional Entertainment Centre and has nine more films spanning drama, animation, comedy and family-friendly favourites on show until March 29.

Alliance Francaise de Perth general manager Alexandre Allais said they were delighted to bring the very best of French cinema to Bunbury and the South West.

“There’s a strong appetite in Bunbury for French film, and it’s important regional audiences have the opportunity to experience world-class cinema and culture close to home,” he said.

“This year’s program combines literary adaptations, standout performances, and a rich mix of genres, from drama and romance to satire and animation, ensuring there’s something for everyone.

“With a selection that includes family-friendly titles, we’re especially excited to encourage families and younger audiences to come along and discover the magic of French cinema.

“We look forward to welcoming film lovers of all ages from across the region to share in this unique celebration.”

title: Magnificent Storm aiming for back-to-back Bunbury Cup wins
plaintext:
Saturday night’s Bunbury Cup will see Magnificent Storm aiming for back-to-back cup wins at Bunbury Trotting Club.

The Group 3 $50,000 harness race has the Ray Williams-trained nine-year-old gelding headlining the field after three wins on the trot, including the Group 3 Pinjarra Cup and Mount Eden Sprint earlier this month.

Among the harness racing the trotting club has plenty of activity for young and old on the day with secretary Sarah Wall saying the night is always one to mark down on the calendar.

“Last year we had a really good turnout,” Wall said.

“With all the events we bring in a great crowd, our restaurant and members are almost fully booked, it’s a great night out for everyone including families.

For the kids there’s also laser tag, bouncy castle, slam dunk basketball, fairy threads and tales, face painting, glitter tattoos, pony trots and train rides.

“We can basically put everything undercover, so all the events will together with a great atmosphere. Once you get in you’ll be able to get away from the elements.”

The club will be revealing its new playground with Wall thankful for the help that was provided.

“Its been in the works for a number of years and not without the support from Don Punch with grants to make it happen, it’s a bonus for families and their kids to have some fun a race night”

title: Kickoff time set for Rams' season opener against 49ers in Australia
plaintext:
The date and kickoff time for the Rams’ season opener in Australia is set.

The NFL announced on Wednesday that the Rams will play the San Francisco 49ers on Friday, Sept. 11, at the Melbourne Cricket Ground at 10:35 a.m. Australian Eastern Time. Because of the time difference, fans in the United States will see the game on Thursday, Sept. 10 at 5:35 p.m. PST.

The league did not announce a broadcast or streaming partner.

It will be the first regular-season NFL game to be played in Australia, where the Rams, Seattle Seahawks, Las Vegas Raiders and Philadelphia Eagles hold global marketing rights.

According to the NFL, “hospitality packages” will be available for purchase through Ticketmaster on April 6, tickets on April 7.

The Rams, who advanced to the NFC championship game last season, are expected to be a favorite to play in Super Bowl LXI at SoFi Stadium.

The Rams have added cornerbacks Trent McDuffie and Jaylen Watson to a roster that includes quarterback Matthew Stafford — the reigning NFL most valuable player — receivers Puka Nacua and Davante Adams and edge rusher Jared Verse among others.

Also on Wednesday, the Rams announced that they re-signed running back Ronnie Rivers to a one-year contract.

Rivers, 27, has been a dependable backup and special teams contributor during his four seasons with the Rams. Last season, he played in 11 games and rushed for 46 yards in nine carries.

title: History made as Leigh Ryswyk becomes first current or former AFL player to come out as gay
plaintext:
Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk has made history, becoming the first current or former openly gay AFL player.

Ryswyk is the second former AFL player to come out as a proud member of the LGBTIQA+ community after former West Coast defender Mitch Brown revealed that he is bisexual in August last year.

Ryswyk played one game for the Lions in 2005 before being delisted, but went on to have a decorated SANFL career with North Adelaide, where he played 226 games between 2006 and 2018.

In his only AFL game, Ryswyk suffered a quad strain against Fremantle and never played again.

Speaking to Joy 94.9’s GayFL on Wednesday, the 41-year-old said this news will come as no surprise to those close to him.

“To people who know me, my closest friends, this is not new,” he said.

“I’ve been out for five years now.

“There will be people though ... it will be the first time they know about this.

“I’m a very private person, so it’s not all over my social media and things like that, and that’s fine.

“It can be a bit of a shock to some people, they might not know, but that’s life, right?

“In the end, the people who are nearest and closest to me know, and that’s what’s most important.”

Asked on-air “why now?”, Ryswyk simply responded: “Why not?”.

title: No Johhny Depp as Perth Film Studios gets modest maiden production starring BAFTA winner Sheridan Smith
plaintext:
After unsubstantiated rumours swirled that Johnny Depp would be the first Hollywood star to grace the new Perth Film Studios, the Cook Government’s $290 million Malaga facility is getting a more modest maiden production.

Award-winning English actor Sheridan Smith is hardly the household name Depp is, but the star of Cilla and Mrs Biggs will arrive in WA fresh off this week’s BAFTA nomination for her performance in the four-part true crime series, I Fought the Law.

Smith is set to star opposite Stephen Peacocke (RFDS) and screen legend Judy Davis (Nitram) in Two Birds, a new mystery thriller co-produced by the UK’s ITV and Stan.

The series has Smith playing British plod Izzie Cornwell, recently widowed and looking for a fresh start in the small fictional Aussie town of Dugdale.

When Izzie is assigned to investigate a murder, the stakes are raised when the victim is revealed to be part of a notorious local crime family, led by the formidable Davis’ character.

British filmmaker James Griffiths, who previously gave us the highly regarded Ballad of Wallis Island, has been tapped to direct and will serve as one of the executive producers.

Filming is due to begin soon in Perth and Kalgoorlie, as well as on one of Perth Film Studios four soundstages.

title: Beloved annual Lower Kalgan Pumpkin Festival cancelled this weekend due to unfavourable weather forecast
plaintext:
The beloved Lower Kalgan Pumpkin Festival scheduled to take place this weekend was unfortunately cancelled by organisers due to the unfavourable weather forecast.

The organising committee made the decision to cancel this years event in the interest of ensuring the safety and wellbeing of all participants, volunteers and visitors.

While the pumpkin festival won’t proceed, the pumpkin committee has confirmed they will still conduct the weighing of the heaviest pumpkin entries, albeit on a smaller scale.

A presentation evening and pumpkin soup gathering will be held on May 16 at the Lower Kalgan Hall.

Pumpkin festival secretary Jen Kaz said it was disappointing, but was looking forward to the event later in May.

“We put in a lot of time and there was lots of effort from volunteers so it is a bit upsetting,” she said.

“We still want to do the pumpkin weigh-in presentations on May 16.”

To keep updated, check the Lower Kalgan Community Association website and Facebook page.

title: Oil at $US150 will trigger global recession, BlackRock boss Larry Fink warns
plaintext:
A surge in oil prices to as high as $US150 a barrel will trigger a global recession, the boss of the world’s biggest fund manager, BlackRock, has warned.

Larry Fink said the outlook for oil prices hinged on how the conflict in the Middle East evolved.

“If Iran remains a threat and oil prices stay high, it will have profound implications for the world economy,” he told the BBC.

He cautioned that a prolonged period of elevated crude could result in “a probably stark and steep recession”.

But Mr Fink reckons it is still too early to determine the ultimate scale and outcome of the conflict.

He outlined two possible extreme scenarios: a de-escalation that allows Iran to re-integrate into the global economy could push oil prices below pre-conflict levels, while a prolonged standoff could lead to “years of above $US100, closer to $US150 oil”.

Oil markets have already tightened significantly. The February 28 air strikes on Iran caused crude oil prices to shoot above $US100 a barrel for the first time in four years, resulting in motorists paying more than $2.50 for unleaded petrol.

If oil prices were to rise to $US150 for three or four years, “you would have so many countries moving so rapidly towards solar and maybe even wind”, Mr Fink said.

Countries should not depend on just one source, he said.

“Use what you have unquestionably, but also aggressively move towards alternative sources too.”

title: E-bike rider rushed to Royal Perth Hospital following crash at intersection of Milligan and Murray Street
plaintext:
An e-bike rider has been rushed to hospital after he was struck by a Toyota HiLux at a busy intersection in the Perth CBD.

The 38-year-old male rider suffered serious injuries and was rushed to Royal Perth Hospital after colliding with the car at the intersection of Milligan and Murray Streets about 9.25am on Wednesday.

The 42-year-old driver of the HiLux was uninjured.

Police are investigating the crash and want to hear from anyone who filmed, or witnessed, either of the vehicles prior to the crash.

Police are urging anyone with information to contact crime stoppers on 1800 333 000 or online.

title: Lotto winner in Perth’southern suburbs claims $15 million division one jackpot
plaintext:
A Lotto winner from Perth’s south almost missed the phone call of a lifetime, mistaking news of his $15 million jackpot for a cold call.

The hospitality worker scored the entire division one prize in last week’s Oz Lotto draw after hurriedly buying a ticket during a busy day at work.

The man, who is aged in his 40s, said he would usually have played his favourite numbers but instead bought a Slikpik.

“I had customers knocking at the door so I just picked a ticket with random numbers as I was in a rush,” he said.

“I didn’t even think to check my ticket after the draw since they weren’t my usual numbers.”

After winning the jackpot, Lotterywest attempted to phone him with the life-changing news but the man instead initially ignored the calls.

The man plans to spend his newfound riches on a house as well as an upgraded car and a European cruise from his family.

When asked whether he plans to keep working he said: “Mentally yes, but physically no.”

Lotterywest spokesperson Zoe Wender said the man registered his ticket which meant he could be contacted with the news of his win — even if he took a while to answer.

“It’s a great day in the office when we can make that call and share such fantastic news with a WA player,” she said.  “There’s that moment of disbelief, often followed by confusion, and then the penny drops.  “If you could bottle that moment of realisation and joy, it would be a guaranteed bestseller.”

“I didn’t even think to check my ticket after the draw since they weren’t my usual numbers.”

After winning the jackpot, Lotterywest attempted to phone him with the life-changing news but the man instead initially ignored the calls.

The man plans to spend his newfound riches on a house as well as an upgraded car and a European cruise from his family.

When asked whether he plans to keep working he said: “Mentally yes, but physically no.”

Lotterywest spokesperson Zoe Wender said the man registered his ticket which meant he could be contacted with the news of his win — even if he took a while to answer.

“It’s a great day in the office when we can make that call and share such fantastic news with a WA player,” she said.  “There’s that moment of disbelief, often followed by confusion, and then the penny drops.  “If you could bottle that moment of realisation and joy, it would be a guaranteed bestseller.”

title: Man dies in police custody after retirement-home arrest
plaintext:
A man has died in custody after allegedly struggling to breath in the back of a police van.

He had been arrested "without incident" after officers responded to reports a man had damaged window louvres and assaulted a woman at a retirement village in Darwin's south about 8.40pm on Tuesday.

"The man was secured in the rear cage of a police vehicle," police said in a statement.

"A short time later the man was observed by members to have laboured breathing."

Officers began CPR before paramedics arrived, taking the man to Royal Darwin Hospital where he was declared deceased about 10:10pm.

"The formal cause of death is yet to be determined and the deceased is yet to be formally identified," the statement said.

A crime scene has been established at the police vehicle and the arrest's location.

The death is being investigated by the NT Police's major crime section detectives with oversight from the Professional Standards Command.

"Police are also investigating this matter on behalf of the coroner," the statement said.

title: Women-only weekend in the snow
plaintext:
Skiing can be daunting for those still perfecting the art, and in mixed company.

And that precisely explains why the Perisher Ski Resort town in NSW’s Snowy Mountains, in Kosciuszko National Park, has come up with the Women’s Ski Weekend.

The new, two-day experience aims to build confidence and let women enjoy more of the mountain in good company.

It will be led by Perisher’s expert female instructors on August 15 and 16, 2026. And it is $549 per person (participants must also have a valid lift ticket).

The weekend is aimed at intermediate to advanced skiers, with six-hour sessions each day. The price also includes a welcome breakfast, lunch both days and Saturday apres-ski with a glass of champagne.

A spokesperson for the Women’s Ski Weekend team says: “It is designed for lower-intermediate to advanced skiers who want to elevate their skills in a supportive, empowering environment.

“With expert-led sessions from our top female ski instructors, you’ll learn the tips and tricks to refine your technique, boost confidence, and explore exciting new terrain.

“Connect with like-minded women, enjoy a vibrant mix of on-snow coaching and social events, and take on the mountain with the guidance of instructors who know how to help you progress and conquer any fears — all while having an unforgettable weekend on the slopes.”

perisher.com.au

title: Israeli army says it killed Irans Quds Force member in Beirut strike
plaintext:
The Israeli army spokesperson, Avichay Adraee, said that the military and the Shin Bet killed a member of Iran's Quds Force in a strike in Beirut on Monday.

In a statement on X, Adraee said the navy carried out the strike on Monday based on intelligence provided by Shin Bet, targeting a man identified as Mohammed Ali Kourani.

The statement alleged that Kourani was involved in promoting what it described as terrorist plots under the direction of Iranian intelligence.

"Iranian intelligence agencies continue to operate from within Lebanon by recruiting and directing individuals to carry out attacks," he said.

title: Iranian missile intercepted over Lebanese airspace for first time by foreign warship, sources told Reuters
plaintext:
Three senior Lebanese security sources told Reuters on Tuesday that an Iran missile was intercepted over Lebanon airspace for the first time, with two sources saying a foreign warship carried out the interception.

The Lebanese news agency reported that fragments from the missile fell on several towns north of Beirut, causing minor injuries.

title: Cultural performances and dishes to wrap up Kalgoorlie Primary School Harmony Week
plaintext:
Students at Kalgoorlie Primary School were decked out in orange to wrap up their Harmony Week celebrations last Friday.

Families were invited to an assembly of cultural performances before sharing a picnic of dishes from across the world.

Teacher Bianca Mitchell said about 27 per cent of KPS students were from a country other than Australia.

“It’s big thing in our school community, and we love to celebrate every student wherever they’re from,” she said.

Performances were given by both current and past students at the Harmony assembly and included a Scottish Highland dance and the Maori haka.

“We also had Linda Larbi from the multicultural community share stories from Ghana, which was amazing,” Ms Mitchell said.

“It was probably one of the biggest assemblies we’ve had.”

After the performances, parents and families were invited to stay for “a taste of KPS”.

“We invited families to share their cultural dishes with us,” Ms Mitchell said.

“We had some curries out there, we had a little Welsh store with Welsh cakes, Filipino dishes, all sorts,” she said.

Ms Mitchell said the students loved the celebrations and were engaged and respectful all week.

“Especially the students from a different country, they’re walking around so much prouder this week,” she said.

“Everyone is asking them questions, or we’re doing different learning activities in the class, and then a big day where everyone can come in and celebrate.”

title: Kenya Airways reports increased flight demand due to the Iran war
plaintext:
Kenya Airways said on Monday demand for seats on its flights had jumped due to the effects of war in the Middle East, with most of the gains coming from Europe, the US and Asia.

The US-Israeli war on Iran has upset the global aviation industry, prompting some airlines to raise fares and change their schedules to reduce stops at airports in the Middle East or to cancel flights altogether.

Kenya Airways said demand for seats on its flights, known as load factor, was now almost at 100%, up from around 70% in January.

“We were like this... until February. Then it significantly increased. We reached up to 90% total, 90, 99,” George Kamal, acting CEO of Kenya Airways, told reporters.

“And so the most we see the gains are coming from Europe, from the US and Asia. Those routes are contributing positively, very positively, to our network now,” Kamal added, without giving any further details.

Kenya Airways has about 56 days of jet fuel supply and is making efforts to obtain more from India, its flight operations head Paul Njoroge said.

Reuters

title: Trump claims calm but war tells a different story
plaintext:
Donald Trump's claims of progress clash with ongoing strikes, raising doubts about truth, strategy and the reality of conflict in the region.

It is a sign of the strange times we live in – and the strange people dominating the news cycle that, as some wag pointed out, we have to ask the Iranians if Donald Trump was telling the truth yesterday when he said there had been talks with Tehran.

The Iranians said no. Do we believe them? No. Do we, however, accept anything that Trump says, given his habit of bending the truth or flip-flopping after the fact? No.

The US president didn’t call a ceasefire – but said the US would not attack Iranian power plants as he himself had threatened to do if Tehran did not reopen the Strait of Hormuz to shipping.

His apparent climb-down came just hours before that deadline was to expire.

Tehran said Trump is saying this only to calm the global oil markets, which have gone crazy in the past four weeks. There may be some truth in that.

But if Trump is trying to put together a graceful withdrawal, he won’t be able to.

After repeated promises that the Iranians would be destroyed, somebody is still firing missiles and launching drones at Israel and the US allies in the gulf region.

Someone is still fighting the war Trump claims to have won.

title: Identity of man accused of encouraging ex-colleagues to die by suicide and sending their details to funeral homes revealed
plaintext:
The identity of a man accused of giving the details of his former colleagues to funeral homes after encouraging them to die by suicide has been revealed.

Adrian Bruno, 38, is accused of sending “graphic and confronting” emails to some of his former female colleagues.

Some of the threatening emails allegedly encouraged the women to die by suicide, police allege.

Mr Bruno is further accused of giving his alleged victims’ contact details to funeral homes, which then contacted the women to make arrangements for their funeral services.

The 38-year-old fronted Burwood Local Court for the first time on Tuesday morning.

Clutching a bible, Mr Bruno asked for an adjournment to seek legal advice.

His request was granted, with his matter to return to court on April 14 when he is due to enter pleas.

“I can leave? I can go home?” Mr Bruno asked the registrar.

Mr Bruno declined to comment on the case to journalists outside court, telling them “I’m seeking legal advice”.

He was arrested on March 16 and charged with 17 counts of using a carriage service to menace/harass/offend, use carriage service with intent for another to commit suicide, publish etc false misleading material to obtain advantage and destroy or damage property.

Mr Bruno will remain on bail ahead of his next court appearance, with interim apprehended violence orders to continue until that date.

title: Texas oil refinery explosion: Valero plant in Port Arthur burns after incident
plaintext:
A major emergency operation is underway in the US after an explosion at a large oil refinery.

An explosion has been reported in Port Arthur, Texas at the Valero refinery.

Valero is the largest global independent petroleum refine, with the Port Arthur operation understood to pump out 435,000 barrels a day. The site refines crude oil, it into petroleum, diesel and jet fuel.

Initially after the blast was reported, authorities confirmed they were yet to reach the site, which is a 90 minute drive east of Houston.

Port Arthur Police and Fire Departments have reportedly told residents in the area to “shelter in place” as they race to assess the situation.

Enormous plumes of black smoke could bee seen billowing into the sky in videos that quickly circulated online.

The explosion could at a horror time for petrol prices, which are currently soaring due to war in the Middle East and the blockage in the Strait of Hormuz.

No explanation for a likely cause of the explosion has been reported.

More to come...

title: Solomon Lew’s Apparel Brands boost Myer’s first-half sales, profit
plaintext:
Department store giant Myer has posted a 21.7 per cent increase in half-year profit, boosted by the integration of the five brands it bought from Solomon Lew’s Premier Investments.

Net profit hit $51.7 million in the 26 weeks to January 24. Reflecting the inclusion of Just Jeans, Portmans, Dotti, Jay Jays and Jacqui E — collectively known as Apparel Brand — statutory net profit rose 32.8 per cent to $40.3m.

Total sales lifted 24.5 per cent to $2.28 billion, which includes the Apparel Brands, but rose just 2.1 per cent on a pro forma basis.

“Sales growth was achieved both in store and online, and our disciplined cost management allowed us to make targeted investments including in e-commerce, marketing, product, merchandise and supply chain to deliver on our plan,” Myer executive chair Olivia Wirth said.

“We achieved our biggest Black Friday on record for Myer retail, and total sales for the group through the important trading months of December and January were in line with last year — a good outcome that demonstrates the resilience of the business.”

In a trading update, Myer said total sales for the group rose 1.7 per cent in the first seven weeks of the second half with its department store reporting sales growth of 2.2 per cent.

Myer’s Apparel Brands reported a 0.4 per cent rise in sales. Just Jeans posted sales growth of 9.8 per cent.

title: Victorian teachers’ strike: 30k walk out demanding 35 per cent pay rise
plaintext:
Tens of thousands of teachers are trading the classroom for the picket line on Tuesday in “unprecedented” industrial action.

For the first time in more than a decade, Victorian teachers are staging a major walkout, with more than 30,000 public school workers set to take part in strikes.

It has left more than 500 schools in the state operating on skeleton staff, as educators continue to fight the state government over pay rates.

They are attempting to push for a 35 per cent pay increase as well as other improvements to school working conditions.

And the Australian Education Union has warned that the disruption could continue beyond Tuesday’s unprecedented day of action.

“We will escalate our campaign,” AEU Victorian branch president Justin Mullaly told ABC Radio.

“They (the government) need to step up. The Premier needs to step in and actually put an offer on the table that will resolve the dispute and will value school staff.

“We know there are too many people in our schools that struggle to make ends meet. There are education support staff who have multiple jobs.”

On Monday, reports suggested some schools were encouraging parents to keep their children at home in anticipation of the strikes.

However, the state government website does not advise of any school closures in any region as a result.

It is the first time teachers in Victoria have staged a walkout since 2013.

title: USS Gerald Ford back at Crete naval base: AFP
plaintext:
The USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier that has been part of Middle East war operations, on Monday returned to a naval base in Crete, an AFP photographer said.

The vessel, which took on food, fuel, and ammunition at Souda Bay in February, reported a laundry fire on March 12, which injured two crew members.

title: Police nab man with R8m cocaine in backpack in Umhlanga complex
plaintext:
Police arrested a man carrying R8m worth of cocaine in his backpack at a residential complex in Somerset Park, Umhlanga, on Monday.

Provincial spokesperson Sgt Thenjiswa Ngcobo said police received information about a man who was dealing in drugs and kept watch on him at his house.

“Just after midnight on Monday morning, the suspect was spotted carrying a backpack at a residential complex on Ilchester Avenue, Canterbury, in [the] Somerset Park area.

“The suspect was stopped by the police, and upon searching the backpack, a substance suspected to be gold cocaine was found in his possession.”

Police also seized an undisclosed amount of money and three cellphones from the suspect.

The man was expected to appear in court on Monday.

title: Recipe of the day: Biltong and egg salad

Eggs are one of the easiest ways to boost the nutritional value of everyday meals, including something as simple as a salad.

With South Africans eating far fewer vegetables than recommended, adding eggs to salads is a practical way to create meals that are both more satisfying and nutritious.

title: Oryon Cell Therapies gets new funding for Parkinson’s treatment production
plaintext:
Oryon Cell Therapies emerged from stealth on Monday with $21m (about R362m) in new funding and early clinical data showing signs that its experimental therapy may help restore lost brain function in Parkinson’s patients.

The new tranche brings Oryon’s total funding to $42m.

Here are some details:

title: Phillies pitcher suffers cardiac event after bullpen session at spring training
plaintext:
Philadelphia Phillies pitcher Daniel Robert suffered a serious medical emergency while at the team’s training facility in Florida on Sunday morning.

Robert was at BayCare Ballpark in Clearwater when he suffered a cardiac event, according to MLB.com. He was on the pitcher’s mound next to the half-field after he completed his first bullpen session since October.

In October, Robert also had a cardiac event. Doctors inserted an implantable cardioverter-defibrillator (ICD) near his chest over the winter. The device triggered when he was leaving the mound on Sunday, according to the report.

"I was standing right behind him," manager Rob Thomson said afterward. "It was scary because he went down, he started to get back up again and he went back down."

He added that it was "very scary."

Robert was taken to the hospital and was released hours after the incident.

The 31-year-old pitcher was taken in the 21st round of the MLB Amateur Draft in 2017 by the Texas Rangers. He made four appearances for the Rangers in 2024. He had six strikeouts in 5.2 innings.

Robert was traded to the Phillies in April 2025 for minor leaguer Enrique Segura. He made 15 appearances for Philadelphia, striking out 15 batters and allowing seven runs – six earned – in 13 innings.

It’s unclear how long Robert will be off the mound until he is cleared.

title: Ex-tropical cyclone Narelle: Severe weather warning and cyclone advice alert issued for WA’s north coast
plaintext:
A severe weather warning has been issued for parts of WA’s coast as ex-tropical cyclone Narelle descends on the west, with heavy rainfall, flash flooding, and damaging winds expected.

Narelle is likely to cross the Kimberley coast north of Wyndham this evening, and will continue on its west-southwest trajectory “as a deep tropical low” towards the northwest Kimberley coast on Tuesday morning.

Heavy rainfall, which could lead to flash flooding at the centre of the storm, has been projected by the Bureau of Meteorology, with whopping totals expected.

Between 80 and 100 millimetres could fall, which could peak at 140mm in certain areas.

Damaging winds of up to 65-75km/h are possible, including peak gusts of 90km/h, according to BOM, for the area east of the Mitchell Plateau.

The Department of Fire and Emergency Services advises that people should:

Updates will be available at the Bureau of Meteorology website, or on TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210.

The ex-tropical cyclone has also triggered advice alerts along the northwest coast, as an alert was issued on Monday afternoon for the area between Kuri Bay and Beagle Bay, as well as Wallal Downs in the area west of Port Hedland.

Emergency WA have warned people should begin to prepare for dangerous weather in the coming hours.

title: Kings rally in third period before falling to Mammoth in overtime
plaintext:
SALT LAKE CITY — Nick Schmaltz scored his second goal of the game at 1:46 of overtime to give the Utah Mammoth a 4-3 victory over the Kings on Sunday night.

Schmaltz carried the puck in on a two-on-one rush with defenseman Mikhail Sergachev on his left and fired a snap shot between goalie Darcy Kuemper‘s legs.

After the Kings controlled play in the three-on-three overtime and nearly ended it on Alex Laferriere’s shot that went off the right post, Kevin Stenlund won a faceoff against Quinton Byfield to set up Schmaltz’s seventh winning goal of the season.

Artemi Panarin tied it for the Kings with 3:30 left in regulation with his 25th goal of the season. His shot from deep on the far right side eluded goalie Karel Vejmelka.

Schmaltz pushed his career-high goals total to 26 and reached 63 points to match his career high set in 2024-25. Lawson Crouse scored twice in the first period and added an assist, and Vejmelka made 33 saves to help Utah end a four-game home losing streak. The Mammoth hold the first wild card in the Western Conference, five points ahead of Nashville.

Byfield had a goal and an assist, and Laferriere also scored for the Kings. Kuemper stopped 30 shots.

The Kings are two points behind Nashville for the final wild-card spot.

title: Former Google executive Matt Brittin named new BBC director-general, The Times reports
plaintext:
March 22 (Reuters) - Former Google executive Matt Brittin has been confirmed as the BBC's new director-general, the Times reported on Sunday, citing sources.

Brittin's appointment was approved by the BBC board at a meeting on Thursday, with an official announcement expected this week, the report said.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report. BBC did not immediately respond to a request for a comment.

In January, BBC said Director-General Tim Davie would step down on April 2 and be temporarily succeeded by Rhodri Talfan Davies, the broadcaster's director of nations, until a permanent replacement is appointed.

Davie announced his resignation in Novemberfollowing criticism of the British corporation's news service over the misleading editing of a video clip of U.S. President Donald Trump.

(Reporting by Angela Christy in Bengaluru; Editing by Rashmi Aich and Sumana Nandy)

title: Photos: Butterfly Pavilion nets wide-eyed visitors
plaintext: Nature lovers, rejoice! The Natural History Museum’s Butterfly Pavilion is back, with up to 30 butterfly and moth species, as well as an assortment of California plants. The seasonal outdoor exhibit, which runs through Aug. 23, allows visitors to see nature up close — we’re talking walking amid hundreds of butterflies and having them land on your arms or shoulders. A $10 add-on ticket along with a museum ticket is required to explore the pavilion. Visits last half an hour.

title: Lebanon Disaster Risk Management Unit says Karantina site is precautionary, not for immediate use
plaintext:
The Disaster Risk Management Unit said that reports circulating about a site being prepared in Karantina are not accurate.

The unit explained that the site is being prepared as a precautionary measure and not for immediate use, noting that its intended purpose has not yet been determined.

It also confirmed that the site is located outside the Port of Beirut area and does not affect operations there in any way.

The site is about one kilometer from residential neighborhoods, and if work begins there, it will be accompanied by the necessary measures to maintain public safety and good relations with the local community.

The area is also under the supervision of the Lebanese army and relevant security forces.

The unit also stressed the need for responsible and calm discourse on the issue, away from misinformation and exaggeration, in order to protect the public interest, national unity and the country’s stability.

title: Wundowie: Man trapped in vehicle after early-morning collision on Great Eastern Highway
plaintext:
One man is trapped after a high speed collision on a busy highway east of Perth.

A rescue helicopter and three ambulance crews are responding to the crash which happened just after 6am Monday morning on Great Eastern Highway in Wundowie, which is halfway between Mundaring and Northam.

One person is confirmed to have serious injuries from the collision.

A witness described an “absolutely horrific” scene.

He told 6PR two cars appeared to have collided head-on on the highway near the Hawke Avenue turnoff.

The crash involved a Commodore ute and an unknown white car.

More to come.

title: Galaxy can't capitalize on early Portland red card in draw with Timbers
plaintext:
PORTLAND, Ore. — Portland’s Kristoffer Velde scored in the 13th minute and Timbers defender Kamal Miller picked up a red card seven minutes later, but James Pantemis surrendered only a João Klauss goal to help them hold on for a 1-1 draw with the Galaxy on Sunday.

Velde scored for the second time this season to give Portland (1-3-1) a 1-0 lead early. But things became difficult from the 20th minute on when Miller received his card for a foul on Klauss.

Antony Alves Santos notched his first assist this season on the score and Joao Ortiz picked up his second.

Klauss came up with the equalizer in the 30th minute with assists from Marco Reus and Gabriel Pec. It was the fifth goal for Klauss, who has certainly helped ease the loss of superstar Riqui Puig for a second straight season because of injuries. Klauss spent his first three seasons with St. Louis City, where he scored 25 goals in 79 appearances.

Reus earned his first assist this season after posting a career-best nine last year. Pec’s helper was his third to begin the season.

Pantemis totaled six saves for the Timbers, including four in the first half.

JT Marcinkowski stopped two shots in his first start of the season for the Galaxy (1-2-2).

The Galaxy lead the series 14-12-11, but are 5-10-11 in Portland. The two clubs played to a 1-1 draw in Portland last season before the Timbers posted a 4-2 victory on the road.

Up next for the Galaxy: vs. Minnesota at Dignity Health Sports Park on April 4.

title: Man allegedly stabbed at Newcastle bowling club, another man arrested
plaintext:
A man has allegedly been stabbed at a bowling club in Newcastle, with another man charged with attempted murder.

Police were called to the Merewether Bowling Club about 7.40pm on Sunday after reports of a stabbing.

A man had allegedly been stabbed several times near the club’s entrance by a man believed to be known to him, police say.

The man, aged 69, was treated at the scene before paramedics arrived.

He was taken to the John Hunter Hospital in a serious but stable condition, with stab wounds to his chest and back.

A 53-year-old man was arrested at a home in Stockton about 1.40am on Monday.

He was charged with attempt murder (DV) and contravene prohibition/restriction in AVO (domestic).

He was refused bail to appear before a bail court on Monday.

More to come

title: Mental health support during war: Helping children cope with fear and loss
plaintext:
Attention to mental health is critical, especially under the current war conditions. Guidance has been provided on how to support children in shelters and what to tell those who are frightened by the sounds of sonic booms and airstrikes.

The loss of a family member — sometimes even both parents — is another tragedy of war that must be addressed with children honestly and openly.

Adults and parents also need to take care of their own mental health. Fear is not shameful, and admitting fear is not shameful either.

In difficult circumstances, people are not expected to apply all recommended steps, but applying even one can help reduce the psychological impact of war.

Mental health is one of the least discussed topics during this period, for both children and adults. Guidance emphasizes ways to maintain mental resilience and reduce psychological risks for children.
sentence: In difficult circumstances, people are not expected to apply all recommended steps, but applying even one can help reduce the psychological impact of war.

title: Iranian man, Romanian woman charged over attempt to enter UK submarine base
plaintext:
An Iranian man and a Romanian woman have been charged after attempting to enter Britain's nuclear submarine base in Scotland, Police Scotland said on Saturday.

The two, who British media have called suspected Iranian spies, ⁠were arrested on Thursday.

The charges come three weeks into the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran. While Britain has not taken part in the attacks on Iran, the country's forces have downed Iranian missiles and drones in the Gulf ⁠region.

HM Naval Base Clyde is located on the west coast of Scotland and is key to Britain's security, ⁠hosting the country's nuclear-armed submarine fleet, as well as its attack submarines.

Police Scotland said ⁠the Iranian man, 34, and the Romanian woman, 31, are ⁠due to appear at Dumbarton Sheriff Court on March 23.

title: Venezuelan migrant arrested after Loyola Chicago student fatally shot near campus
plaintext:
A 25-year-old man has been arrested in connection with the death of a Loyola University Chicago student shot near campus Thursday by a masked gunman, the Chicago Tribune reported.

The Tribune said it had obtained the arrest report and was not naming the suspect, who sources tell Fox News is a migrant from Venezuela.

Sheridan Gorman, 18, of Westchester County, New York, was gunned down while taking a walk with friends shortly after 1:30 a.m. Thursday along Chicago's lakefront.

The gunman allegedly approached the group, pulled out a gun, and opened fire — striking Gorman in the head, according to the Chicago Police Department. She was pronounced dead at the scene.

No other injuries were reported, according to police.

Records obtained by the Tribune state the suspect was identified minutes after the shooting due to his "distinct limp."

In a letter sent to students Thursday, Loyola University Chicago President Mark C. Reed confirmed Gorman’s death while addressing the campus community.

"It is with profound sadness that I write to share that one of our students, Sheridan Gorman, was killed earlier today," Reed said. "This is a tragic loss, and our hearts go out to Sheridan’s family, loved ones, and all who knew her."

title: STM Loves: Owston Hotel, Taittinger on the Terrace and a collaboration between ROSÉ and Levi’s
plaintext:
With long-lunch favourite Vin Populi, delicious Common Bakery and delightful Ode to Sirens, we didn’t need another reason to head to Fremantle’s West End, but here is one anyway. Owston Hotel has opened on High Street, with the boutique 11-room space filled with Australian linen, handmade furniture and personal touches throughout — the perfect place to rest your head after a day of eating and exploring. For more details, or to book, head to owstonhotel.com.

For a taste of Paris in the heart of East Fremantle, a mid-week sip at La Lune should do the trick. Every Wednesday the French bistro is offering a glass of Taittinger NV Brut Prestige for $20 from 4pm, the perfect way to bid au revoir to hump day as a DJ turns relaxed, Euro-inspired tunes. For bookings go to lalunefremantle.com.

The singer, known as part of K-pop group Blackpink and for her solo work (her song APT. with Bruno Mars has been everywhere), has signed on to bring her cool edge to denim stalwarts Levi’s. The collaboration comes after ROSÉ starred in the brand’s recent Behind Every Original campaign, which kicked off at the Super Bowl, and performed in custom jeans at Blackpink’s Tokyo concerts. The singer is also working with the company on new pieces, to be released later this year.

title: Letters to the Editor: Global alliances ‘endure when responsibilities are shared and not avoided’
plaintext:
To the editor: European leaders describe the conflict with Iran as “not our war” (“Ukraine’s aid against Iran shows who the U.S. allies really are,” March 18). A surprising attitude when considering, had the U.S. taken a similar stance in World War I and World War II, Europe’s position would have been far less certain and possibly a different landscape altogether. American intervention was a choice that came at enormous cost in lives, resources and national resolve.

Even today in the Russian war with Ukraine, America is playing a central role in safeguarding Europe, supplying all forms of intelligence and support on the world stage.

Alliances endure when responsibilities are shared and not avoided.

Richard Austen, Westchester

..

To the editor: Guest contributor Mikhail Alexseev’s op-ed offers us the reminder we need. If only the billions of dollars, military assets and support our self-named “president of peace” has thrown at Israel in fighting the Palestinians and Iranians were offered to Ukraine instead. President Trump would be a new hero. Instead, he finds himself in a critical position, with only Israel Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Russian President Vladimir Putin as would-be pals.

June Maguire, Mission Viejo
sentence: Even today in the Russian war with Ukraine, America is playing a central role in safeguarding Europe, supplying all forms of intelligence and support on the world stage.
context:
To the editor: European leaders describe the conflict with Iran as “not our war” (“Ukraine’s aid against Iran shows who the U.S. allies really are,” March 18). A surprising attitude when considering, had the U.S. taken a similar stance in World War I and World War II, Europe’s position would have been far less certain and possibly a different landscape altogether. American intervention was a choice that came at enormous cost in lives, resources and national resolve.

Even today in the Russian war with Ukraine, America is playing a central role in safeguarding Europe, SAFO intelligence and support on the world stage.

Alliances endure when responsibilities are shared and not avoided.

Richard Austen, Westchester

..

To the editor: Guest contributor Mikhail Alexseev’s op-ed offers us the reminder we need. If only the billions of dollars, military assets and support our self-named “president of peace” has thrown at Israel in fighting the Palestinians and Iranians were offered to Ukraine instead. President Trump would be a new hero. Instead, he finds himself in a critical position, with only Israel Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Russian President Vladimir Putin as would-be pals.

June Maguire, Mission Viejo

title: Wimbledon courts a hi-tech upgrade with video review
plaintext:
Wimbledon will use video review technology for the first time at this year's tournament.

The All England Club said on Saturday the oldest grand slam tennis tournament will have the technology available on Centre Court, No.1 Court - the club's second-biggest stadium - and four other show courts.

Players will be allowed to review specific calls made by the chair umpire, such as double bounces.

Video review made its grand slam tennis debut at the 2023 US Open. The Australian Open also uses the technology.

Centre Court and No.1 Court will have video review available throughout the tournament, which starts on June 29, and the technology will be used on No.2 Court, No.3 Court, Court 12 and Court 18 for singles matches.

Players will not be limited in the number of reviews they can request.

Video review is separate from the electronic line-calling used for ruling balls in or out.

Last year Wimbledon replaced line judges with electronic line-calling, though it wasn't without hiccups.

The grass-court major is also adding visual indicators for electronic line-calling on scoreboards showing "out'' and "fault'' calls.

"This enhancement has been made as a result of feedback following the adoption of live electronic line-calling last year," the All England Club said in Saturday's announcement.

plaintext:

This 4-bed, 3-bath sits on a street-to-street lot, offering dual access, privacy and a peaceful setting. title: Fresh twist in Jon Rahm Ryder Cup controversy
plaintext:
Jon Rahm has withdrawn his appeal against sanctions imposed by the DP World Tour for playing conflicting LIV Golf events but is refusing to pay his outstanding fines.

The Press Association understands the Spaniard, who owes more than Â£2million ($A3.8million) in financial penalties, will not be able to compete in DP World Tour events until he settles up.

With the two-time major winner showing no inclination of paying his fines or accepting the conditional release taken by eight other LIV golfers - including his European Team partner Tyrrell Hatton - his participation in next year's Ryder Cup in serious doubt.

Rahm's appeal had allowed him to continue playing and feature in the Ryder Cup win at Bethpage since September 2024.

The deal offered by the DP World Tour was for players to drop appeals, pay outstanding fines and commit to a minimum of six events, two of which were at the tour's choosing.

Rahm claimed that was "extorting players" but the stance taken by the DP World Tour is he signed up to the regulations and was well aware of the implications before he joined LIV.

In April 2023 a Sports Resolutions panel ruled the DP World Tour was within its rights to fine and suspend players who violated the conflicting-events rules.

title: Chuck Norris,  star of ‘Walker, Texas Ranger’, dies at 86
plaintext:
Action star Chuck Norris has died, his family said in a social media post on Friday.

“It is with heavy hearts that our family shares the sudden passing of our beloved Chuck Norris yesterday morning,” Norris’s family said in an Instagram post.

“While we would like to keep the circumstances private, please know that he was surrounded by his family and was at peace. To the world, he was a martial artist, actor and a symbol of strength. To us, he was a devoted husband, a loving father and grandfather, an incredible brother and the heart of our family.

“He lived his life with faith, purpose and an unwavering commitment to the people he loved. Through his work, discipline and kindness, he inspired millions around the world and left a lasting impact on so many lives. While our hearts are broken, we are deeply grateful for the life he lived and for the unforgettable moments we were blessed to share with him,” it continued.

Norris, who died at 86, was an ’80s action-film hero who fought the bad guys in Code of Silence, Missing in Action and The Delta Force and upheld the law in the TV series Walker, Texas Ranger.

The six-time undefeated World Professional Middleweight Karate Champion, whose tough-guy image inspired satirical “facts” that made him an internet phenomenon, had been hospitalised in Hawaii on Thursday, Variety reported.

Reuters

title: Mauritius deputy prime minister quits after disagreement with prime minister
plaintext:
Mauritius’ deputy prime minister Paul Berenger said on Friday he has resigned over what he describes as prime minister Navin Ramgoolam’s failure to appoint a finance minister, a post Ramgoolam doubles in.

Berenger and his Militant Mauritian Movement (MMM) are the main allies of Ramgoolam’s Labour Party in the ruling Alliance du Changement that won elections in November 2024, unseating the incumbent in a vote dominated by a high cost of living crisis.

Berenger told a news conference on Friday that the government had yet to put in place some of its campaign pledges and this, and a failure by Ramgoolam to appoint a stand-alone finance minister, had prompted him to resign.

The risk of a downgrade by ratings agency Moody’s and the possibility of Mauritius being included on a regional Southern and Eastern Africa anti-money laundering grey list also contributed to his decision, Berenger said.

Ramgoolam’s office did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Berenger said he spoke with Ramgoolam late on Thursday and informed him of his intention to quit. He formally submitted his resignation after the news conference. His exit will not lead to the collapse of Ramgoolam’s government.

On Tuesday Ramgoolam said Mauritius expected weaker economic growth and higher inflation this year because of the Middle East conflict.

Reuters

title: Ex-husband of Miss Universe in court accused of fraud
plaintext:
A former Miss Universe Australia's ex-husband has faced court on 115 charges of fraud over claims he stole almost $500,000 from an employer.

Justin McKeone appeared in Melbourne Magistrates Court on Friday for a brief hearing after being charged earlier in March.

The 38-year-old last year faced a civil claim in the Supreme Court brought by his former employer, Cozmic Sales Pty Ltd, which traded as Rocket Brands.

However, the court confirmed to AAP a judge dismissed the proceedings with the consent of all parties on March 31, 2025.

Victoria Police charged McKeone on March 9 with 115 fraud offences allegedly committed between June and October 2024.

He is accused of dishonestly obtaining for himself a financial advantage by deception namely by transferring himself funds from company funds, court documents stated.

Charge sheets said McKeone allegedly recorded these payments, which he was not entitled to, with a false ledger entries.

The amount of the alleged fraud totalled $481,490.33.

McKeone sat silently in the front row of court as his lawyer Andrew Halphen and prosecutors set upcoming court dates on Friday.

He is charged on summons and will return to the court on June 5 for a committal mention.

McKeone was married to Miss Universe Australia Olivia Molly Rogers, reportedly for eight months, before their split in 2022.

title: The Book Of Mormon musical set to share the good word at Crown Theatre Perth in October
plaintext:
Elder Price and Elder Cunningham would like to share the most amazing book with Perth audiences when musical The Book Of Mormon returns to convert Crown Theatre Perth in October.

The Australian production of this Tony, Olivier and Grammy Award-winning Broadway musical will be back on Perth’s doorstep, with book, music and lyrics by South Park creators Trey Parker and Matt Stone, in collaboration with Avenue Q co-creator Robert Lopez.

The biting satirical musical comedy — not for the easily offended — follows a pair of mismatched Mormon missionaries sent from Salt Lake City to Uganda, with Sean Johnston and Nick Cox cast as Elder Price and Elder Cunningham, respectively.

“We cannot wait to spread the good word,” Cox said about the season announcement.

“Perth’s audiences have been asking, and the Perth audiences shall receive. (We) cannot wait to share this with them.”

While Johnston said he had heard only great things about WA audiences and looked forward to bringing them this incredible production.

“I’ve never been to WA before so I can’t wait to take this iconic show there and see what Perth is all about,” Johnston added.

The Book Of Mormon is at Crown Theatre Perth from October 2026.

title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke heckled in Lakemba Mosque
plaintext:
Australia’s Prime Minister and Home Affairs Minister have made an abrupt exit from a Western Sydney mosque after they were heckled.

Anthony Albanese and Tony Burke had been in Lakemba Mosque when protesters began yelling “Genocide supporters” and “Allah Akbar”.

The two were at the mosque about 7.30am for the Eid prayers with the Lebanese Muslim Association to mark the end of Ramadan.

The association’s secretary Gamel Kheir had been giving a speech to worshippers when the heckling began.

A video from the incident showed several men had attempted to stop the hecklers. One of the individuals was reportedly tackled to the ground and then removed by security.

The PM and Mr Burke had been sitting on the ground surrounded by community members as they watched the protest unfold. They were then escorted out of the prayer hall a short time late.

The outburst, reportedly carried out by Islamic extremists, appeared to be related to the Albanese Government’s response to the Israel-Gaza conflict.

Mr Burke’s Federal seat of Watson includes the Lakemba Mosque, which is Australia’s largest.

More to come.

title: Sydney-bound Delta Air Lines flight hit by turbulence, three hospitalised
plaintext:
Three cabin crew have reportedly been hospitalised after a Sydney-bound flight was hit with violent turbulence.

Emergency crews were waiting on the tarmac as the Delta Air Lines flight touched down at Sydney Airport just after 7am on Friday.

They were alerted to the injuries an hour before the plane, which had departed from Los Angeles, landed.

Eight people, including the three cabin crew, required medical treatment.

It is understood that the turbulence suddenly sent the flight attendants and several passengers into the air.

None of the injuries are believed to be serious.

More to come...

title: Margaret River community gather for opening of the Anne Shepherdson Courtyard named after a beloved icon
plaintext:
The Anne Shepherdson Courtyard at the Margaret River HEART — Nala Bardip Mia was officially opened last weekend, honouring the legacy of one of the region’s most passionate arts champions.

Arts Margaret River and the Shire of Augusta Margaret River unveiled the revitalised courtyard last Sunday, welcoming the community to the new outdoor space.

Taking its name from order of Australia medal recipient Anne Shepherdson, the area honours her decades-long dedication that helped establish a thriving arts culture.

Shepherdson was a cornerstone of the Margaret River community and one of the founding figures of Arts Margaret River.

The event drew a strong turnout from across the community, reflecting the deep respect and affection held for Shepherdson and her lasting contribution to the region’s arts and cultural life.

Shire president Julia Meldrum said Shepherdson’s passing in 2024 was deeply felt throughout the community, and the shire was proud to honour someone who made the region a better place to live.

“Establishing the Anne Shepherdson Courtyard at Nala Bardip Mia ensures her legacy lives on at the shire’s most significant cultural space, while the upgrades to the space itself add to the versatility of the venue,” she said.

Following the courtyard opening, a special Arts Margaret River Sunday sundowner transformed the grassed area outside the HEART into a vibrant pop-up festival of circus and music.

title: RFDS Auxiliary raises funds to buy vital equipment for Kalgoorlie-Boulder base
plaintext:
The RFDS Eastern Goldfields Auxiliary has been working hard to raise funds to buy vital equipment for the Royal Flying Doctor Service’s Kalgoorlie base.

President Gemma Abbott said the auxiliary was “thrilled” to be able to buy a Credo Cube temperature-controlled medical transport bag.

Ms Abbot said the Credo Cube allowed vaccinations to be kept at the required temperature for travel on primary health clinic runs to stations and communities across the Goldfields.

“We also purchased two extra sets of Bose Aviation headsets to be used on the aircraft,” she said.

She said the total cost of these purchases was $6410.

“We could not have made these purchases without the generosity of the Goldfields community who donate to us though tins at spring festival and at businesses throughout Goldfields towns, raffles, and events we run such as the quiz night,” she said.

“Our next event will be Friday, May 8 to celebrate Royal Flying Doctor Day.

“We will be holding a raffle outside Coles Kalgoorlie.”
sentence: “We also purchased two extra sets of Bose Aviation headsets to be used on the aircraft,” she said.
context:
The RFDS Eastern Goldfields Auxiliary has been working hard to raise funds to buy vital equipment for the Royal Flying Doctor Service’s Kalgoorlie base.

President Gemma Abbott said the auxiliary was “thrilled” to be able to buy a Credo Cube temperature-controlled medical transport bag.

Ms Abbot said the Credo Cube allowed vaccinations to be kept at the required temperature for travel on primary health clinic runs to stations and communities across the Goldfields.

“We also purchased TESO Bose Aviation headsets to be used on the aircraft,” she said.

She said the total cost of these purchases was $6410.

“We could not have made these purchases without the generosity of the Goldfields community who donate to us though tins at spring festival and at businesses throughout Goldfields towns, raffles, and events we run such as the quiz night,” she said.

“Our next event will be Friday, May 8 to celebrate Royal Flying Doctor Day.

“We will be holding a raffle outside Coles Kalgoorlie.”

title: Geraldton Bendigo Bank Cricket grand final to hit the airwaves for the first time in league history
plaintext:
For the first time in its history, the Geraldton Bendigo Bank Cricket league men’s A-grade grand final will be on the airwaves this weekend.

The 50-over decider between Sportsmans and Wanderers will be broadcast live on Radio MAMA and Radio Geraldton.

Local cricket expert and statistician Victor Tanti will lead the broadcast, alongside a team of local men’s and women’s players, coaches and local cricket experts.

“It’s great that Pete Fiorenza has been able to make the broadcast happen,” Tanti said.

“Not everyone can get to the game and with Radio Geraldton being available on the net, people anywhere can tune in.”

The broadcast will be live from 11am, following the simulcast of Radio MAMA’s Sports Shed Saturday, which starts at 9am.

“It is part of our aim to promote the Geraldton community live and local,” Radio Geraldton founder and City of Greater Geraldton councillor Peter Fiorenza said.

“It’s great to work with our good friends Radio MAMA.”

title: WATCH | Crackdown on undocumented foreigners nets 15
plaintext:
Police arrested 15 foreigners and a factory owner who employed them during a raid on Wednesday.

Provincial spokesperson Col Robert Netshiunda said the crackdown on illegal employment of undocumented foreigners, carried out in conjunction with department of employment and labour inspectors and immigration officials from home affairs, was conducted in Chatsworth.

“During the operation, five factories were inspected and one factory was found to have illegally employed 15 foreign nationals.

“The operation was a continuation of operations aimed at stamping the authority of the state by ensuring that there is no lawlessness in the business sector which directly or indirectly compromises the livelihood of South Africans.”

title: Suspect arrested in R230k Eastern Cape drug bust due in court
plaintext:
A 37-year-old suspect arrested in the Eastern Cape for possession of drugs worth R230,000 is due to appear in court on Thursday.

Off-duty police officers attached to Nxuba visible policing and K9 unit members nabbed the suspect on Tuesday in Michausdal, Nxuba.

“The team acted on information received about a suspect allegedly in possession of drugs at a local address,” police spokesperson Captain Yolisa Mgolodela said.

“Acting swiftly, the members proceeded to the identified premises where they conducted a search. The suspect cooperated with police and led them to a location within the residence where Mandrax tablets were concealed. The drugs, with an estimated street value of more than R230,000, were confiscated.”

The suspect is due to appear before the Nxuba magistrate’s Court on Thursday on charges of possession of drugs.

District commissioner Maj-Gen Rudolph Adolph praised the members for their vigilance and commitment.

Adolph reiterated that drug trafficking continues to undermine efforts to combat crime in the district.

title: Ukraine peace talks on hold due to Iran war, Russia’s Izvestia reports
plaintext:
Peace talks between the US, Russia and Ukraine are on pause amid the Iran war, newspaper Izvestia reported on Thursday, citing Russian officials.

Izvestia said the Kremlin had confirmed the pause and that the Iran war could push Kyiv towards compromise.

Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Russian presidential envoy Kirill Dmitriev will continue working on investment and economic co-operation but the trilateral talks were on pause.

“Kirill Dmitriev continues work. The trilateral group is on pause,” Peskov was quoted as saying by Izvestia.

Reuters
sentence: “Kirill Dmitriev continues work.
context:

Izvestia said the Kremlin had confirmed the pause and that the Iran war could push Kyiv towards compromise.

Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Russian presidential envoy Kirill Dmitriev will continue working on investment and economic co-operation but the trilateral talks were on pause.

“KDCW. The trilateral group is on pause,” Peskov was quoted as saying by Izvestia.

title: Dunsborough Fun Park hits market for offers above $3m
plaintext:
Dunsborough Fun Park has hit the market, offering a multimillion-dollar opportunity to take over one of the region’s most recognisable leisure sites.

The attraction, which changed hands in 2024, has experienced a stop-start history over the years, cycling through periods of closure, reopening and refurbishment.

The one-hectare property is being sold by JMW Real Estate, advertised for offers above $3m, with the option to continue operating the existing business.

JMW Real Estate property consultant Rob Farris said the fun park had attracted significant interest within days of it being listed.

“The enquiry has been very strong, there is a lot you can do with that unique zoning,” he said.

The future owner also has the option to lease the commercial facilities to an operator or live onsite without operating the business at all.

Along with waterslides, aquatic recreation infrastructure and a licensed restaurant, the property also features a four-bedroom house.

Dunsborough Fun Park has been contacted for comment.

title: WAFL 2025: Perth land former Sydney VFL forward Jack Kluske on the eve of round one
plaintext:
Perth have added further depth to their list on the eve of the WAFL season, signing Sydney VFL forward Jack Kluske.

Kluske bolsters an improved Demons list featuring additions in former Collingwood defender Charlie Dean, ex-Sydney wingman Indhi Kirk and Sandringham’s Freddy Brayshaw.

A half-forward, Kluske kicked 14 goals and averaged 10 touches for the Swans’ reserves team last year.

Kluske is originally from South Australia, having played 36 SANFL games for Port Adelaide’s reserves and Glenelg between 2018 and 2024.

The 27-year-old will arrive at the club this week and is a chance to play in their pre-season clash against Swan Districts at Mineral Resources Park on Saturday.

Perth are building a strong list on the back of a great first-year under coach Mark Stone, having won their first final in 28 years when they beat East Fremantle before losing to Claremont in the first semifinal.

Co-captain Charlie Constable was rewarded for a career best-season when he won the Sandover Medal, while Geelong premiership midfielder Brandan Parfitt is set for a great season year at the Demons.

Parfitt won the Chaney Medal as best on ground in the Northern Territory Football League grand final at the weekend, leading his junior club Nightcliff to victory over St Mary’s.

The former Cat had 27 touches, seven tackles, seven inside-50s and five clearances in a dominant display.

Perth will begin their season against Lathlain co-tenants West Coast on Friday, April 3.

title: ASX opens down sharply following Wall Street losses
plaintext:
The Australian sharemarket has opened into steep negative territory following losses on Wall Street.

The ASX 200 fell 1.6 per cent in the first half-hour of trading on Thursday, as the broader All Ords lost 1.7 per cent.

These immediate losses are in line with dips on the Dow Jones, Nasdaq and S&P500 in the US.

Australian mining and resources stocks have taken the brunt of early losses on escalating warfare in the Middle East. Gold miners in particular have lost value after the US central bank held interest rates.

Missiles flying across Middle Eastern borders mean returns for investors in oil stocks, with the ASX energy sector the only sector to open in positive territory on Thursday.

On the whole, the early morning dip for the ASX 200 wipes about $50bn of value off the market.

More to come
sentence: These immediate losses are in line with dips on the Dow Jones, Nasdaq and S&P500 in the US.

title: Australian stock market: Worrying new strikes on Middle East gas plants send oil prices higher, hammer shares
plaintext:
Australian shares have been pummelled as escalating attacks on major gas facilities in the Middle East triggered a surge in oil prices and increased the threat of protracted pain at the petrol pump.

The S&P-ASX200 index was 1.6 per cent lower at 8506.3 points at 7.35am and on track for its lowest close in four months. Energy was the only sector to gain ground, up 2 per cent as Woodside Energy added 3.5 per cent and Santos 3.4 per cent.

The market’s sharp retreat came after oil prices rose 4 per cent overnight on damaging Iranian retaliatory attacks on the world’s biggest LNG export plant in Qatar.

Iran earlier had warned of retaliation on regional oil and gas infrastructure after its own huge South Pars gas project was struck.

Qatar said its Las Raffan complex, which suspended LNG exports soon after the war erupted in late-February, had suffered “extensive damage”.

The latest attacks jolted energy markets, with traders now fearful that damaged regional oil and gas production facilities that provide 20 per cent of the world’s energy needs could take years to resume pre-war levels.

More to come.
sentence: Qatar said its Las Raffan complex, which suspended LNG exports soon after the war erupted in late-February, had suffered “extensive damage”.

title: Mexican man attempts to cross illegally into US via underside of bridge
plaintext:
A Mexican national was arrested after climbing beneath a busy El Paso overpass near the U.S.-Mexico border, according to video released Tuesday.

The incident happened near Loop 375, close to the border wall in the Bridge of the Americas area. The man maneuvered himself beneath the bridge, moving along support beams above active traffic.

Authorities responded and arrested the individual, U.S. Customs and Border Protection said.

The Bridge of the Americas is a high-traffic crossing point for both vehicles and pedestrians near the U.S.-Mexico border.

It’s not the first time migrants have used this tactic in the area. In November 2025, two individuals climbed beneath the same overpass, navigating between concrete support beams using wooden planks, according to a local outlet.

The latest incident highlights ongoing public safety concerns for both migrants and drivers, as some attempt increasingly risky methods to enter the U.S.

U.S. Customs and Border Protection said on Instagram that agents remain vigilant as illegal crossing tactics evolve.

"CBP Officers remain vigilant in the face of evolving and dangerous attempts to enter the United States unlawfully. Strong borders are the foundation of National Security," the agency said.

title: ‘Rihanna wethu’: Mixed reactions as Jessica Nkosi posts maternity photos
plaintext:
Third baby on the way?

Actress Jessica Nkosi sparked mixed reactions after posting pregnancy photos in her first Instagram post of the year.

In a cryptic caption, the actress did not confirm whether she is expecting another baby, prompting some fans to believe the pictures are old.

“Hey my loves… Happy New Year,” she wrote, adding heart and laughing emojis.

Celebrities such as DJ Zinhle, LootLove and Wizeman Zitha, along with fans, have since taken to the comments section to congratulate the former Isibaya star.

“Rihanna wethu,” one fan commented.

Another wrote: “All I know is that she’s already given birth.”

Third baby on the way?

The actress and her husband, actor Ntokozo “TK” Dlamini, have two children together.

The couple welcomed their first child,Namisa Dlamini, in 2018. In 2023, they announced another pregnancy.

Jessica and TK reportedly tied the knot in 2023. The pair had their umembeso, a traditional Zulu ceremony, on 23 December 2023, where they were seen in traditional attire.

Multiple pictures from the ceremony were shared on social media, although the couple did not post them on their personal accounts.
sentence: In 2023, they announced another pregnancy.

title: Perth players dominate as the women’s Dawn Wellman Memorial Event draws a crowd to Albany’s Stirling Club
plaintext:
The 2026 Dawn Wellman Open Ladies Singles Memorial Event had a stellar turnout with players from Albany, Bunbury and Perth honouring the Albany Eight-Ball Association stalwart at the Stirling Club.

A life member of the association, Ms Wellman died in June last year making the February 21 tournament which bore her name a memorial event.

Originally created to help stay-at-home mums to take a day off to spend with other women, it has grown in stature and attracts many players from outside its home city.

The double elimination format resulted in nine hours of gruelling game play with Perth players Adele Luke and Jess Leabeter holding on to their second lives right up to the quarterfinals.

Bev Mableson, from Albany, and Perth’s Helen Francis Devenish had already burned their spare lives, both losing to Luke in previous games but managed to claw their way into the final rounds.

However, it was the Perth players who dominated the closing stages, Luke beating Leabeter 4-3 in a thriller to reach the grand final.

Devenish overcame Mableson 3-1 to earn a chance against Leabeter for the second slot and made the most of the opportunity to win through 3-0.

The best-of seven final went Luke’s way, taking the grand final 4-1, with Leabeter third overall.

title: Audi A2 to be reborn as entry-level EV
plaintext:
Remember the quirky, all-aluminium Audi A2 that debuted back in 1999? Although it was something of a sales flop, this lightweight MPV has in recent years morphed into a cult favourite, often seen at alternative automotive events like Hagerty’s hipster-baiting Festival of the Unexceptional.

Capitalising on this renewed interest, Audi has announced it will revive the A2 nameplate as an all-electric entry-level model.

Described by CEO Gernot Döllner as “efficient, compact and confident”, the A2 e-tron is said to target a broad international customer base that values everyday urban usability, sustainability, high efficiency and digital connectivity.

Specifications have yet to be revealed. However, a design sketch shows a silhouette very similar to its millennial-era predecessor, suggesting generous interior space and a sizeable boot suited to family life.

The A2 e-tron will be built in Ingolstadt and is set to be unveiled towards the end of the year.

Described by CEO Gernot Döllner as “efficient, compact and confident”, the A2 e-tron is said to target a broad international customer base TVEU usability, sustainability, high efficiency and digital connectivity.

Specifications have yet to be revealed. However, a design sketch shows a silhouette very similar to its millennial-era predecessor, suggesting generous interior space and a sizeable boot suited to family life.

The A2 e-tron will be built in Ingolstadt and is set to be unveiled towards the end of the year.

title: Pick of the Roses: fourth Aussie joins Scottish outfit
plaintext:
Newcastle Jets attacker Lachlan Rose will join Scotland's Dundee United on a two-year deal at the end of the A-League Men's season.

The in-form 26-year-old has signed a pre-contract agreement with The Terrors, with a club-held option of a further 12 months beyond 2028.

Rose is equal-third in the golden boot race with eight goals from 21 games, behind Auckland duo Sam Cosgrove (10) and future teammate Jesse Randall (nine), the New Zealander also signing a pre-contract deal at Tannadice in January.

Rose will link with six-cap Socceroos defender Ryan Strain, former Western Sydney Wanderers striker Zac Sapsford and England-born goalkeeper Ashley Maynard-Brewer as the fourth Australian contracted to United.

Rose is enjoying his most productive seasons at the Jets after signing from Macarthur FC in 2024, matching the goal tally he set last season with five rounds remaining before the ALM finals.

"While we are disappointed to lose Lachie, we are delighted for him that he has earned this opportunity to test himself in Europe," Newcastle's football manager Nigel Boogard said.

"Opportunities like this are what we want for our players and Lachie has put himself in this position through his work and commitment."

Rose has scored 16 goals in 39 games for Newcastle, currently perched one point above Auckland FC, and become something of a cult hero among Novocastrians by helping place the Jets on the cusp of a maiden premiership, having finished runners-up in 2008 and 2018.

title: Aussie podcaster Tobias Nuttall admits to killing sex worker fiancee
plaintext:
A conspiracy theorist podcaster has admitted to killing his sex worker fiancee last year.

Tobias Nuttall pleaded guilty to the murder of Alisha Lauren Hendren-Krippner, 30, who was found with stab wounds at her home on Reid St in Bassendean, in Perth’s east, in August last year.

Ms Hendren-Krippner was rushed to Royal Perth Hospital in a critical condition but died from her injuries.

Nuttall made the admission on Wednesday via video link in the Stirling Gardens Magistrates Court.

Nuttall, 32, was the co-host of a conspiracy theorist podcast called the Waking World where he discussed topics about the occult, magic, spirituality and things clandestine with a British man named Dale.

Ms Hendren-Krippner was a guest on the podcast weeks before her death and talked to Dale about being a sex worker while Nuttall sat mostly quiet during the episode.

She told Dale that she was from New Zealand and moved to Western Australia as a child.

She talked about becoming a stripper at 22, then an escort during Covid and claimed Nuttall taught her about occult practices and sex magic.

Following Nuttall’s arrest, Dale told their listeners that his co-host would no longer be appearing on the show because had been charged with a serious offence in Perth.

“It’s nothing to do with whether we’ve fallen out, it’s much worse than that,” he said.

Nuttall will next appear in the Supreme Court on April 13.

title: Aubin Grove fire: House fire destroys garage and causes extensive damage to property
plaintext:
A home in Perth’s south has extensive damage after a fire erupted just after midnight on Wednesday.

The blaze began in the garage of the property on Nornalup Close in Aubin Grove with firefighters called out to the emergency at 12.05am.

The fire had fully engulfed the garage when emergency services arrived and had started spreading to the back of the home.

More than 10 firefighting crews extinguished the fire by 3am.

Photos from the blaze show the aftermath of the fire with the garage door collapsed and the gutter melted.

There was also extensive damage to the rest of the home.

A smoke alert was issued just before 1am as the fire was causing smoke to bellow onto nearby streets.

Those in the area were urged to remain inside with doors and windows closed as well as air conditioners should also be switched off.

It was all clear around 8.15am.

Department of Fire and Emergency Services are now investigating the cause of the blaze.
